July: Page and Kübler","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:45:14+02:00","pinned":false,"text":["Born on 24 July 2001 in Chartres, Hugo Page is celebrating his 25th birthday on the route of the Tour de France 2026! This is his third consecutive appearance in the race, and he recently achieved his best result to date by finishing 12th in Voiron (stage 17). Hugo Page has one professional victory to his name, secured in a sprint finish on the Tour du Limousin-Périgord – Nouvelle-Aquitaine.
Ferdinand Kübler (1919–2016) was born on 24 July in Marthalen, Switzerland. Turning professional in 1940, he had to wait until the end of the Second World War to demonstrate the full extent of his abilities, which would lead him to become the first Swiss winner of the Tour de France in 1950. He also won eight stages and the points classification (1954), was world road champion (1951), a two-time winner of Liège–Bastogne–Liège (1951–52) and La Flèche Wallonne (also 1951–52), 'Ferdi' is one of the greatest Swiss cyclists in After Orcières-Merlette, the peloton head to another iconic venue: the first mountain top finish in the history of the Tour, and the one that has most often featured! It will be reached after the legendary 13.8 km ascent, with its 21 hairpins and its average gradient of 8.1%.
The peloton are set to roll from Gap at 14:00, to reach km 0 at 14:15. The winners will be crowned at around 17:30."],"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:30:49+02:00","updatedBy":"bvittek","title":"Welcome to the live coverage of stage 19 of the Tour de France 2026","createdBy":"bvittek","id":1353061,"_id":"3083073ecf289dd1f921c5a4af6f002bf3327a37d3539454a40f6f80779dbbf2","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784892650125,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:welcome-to-the-live-coverage-of-stage-19-of-the-tour-de-france-2026","model":"news"},{"createdAt":"2026-07-24T14:03:15+02:00","stageType":"HMG","picto":"liv_green_jersey","slug":"green-jersey-52","locale":"en","updatedBy":"bvittek","title":"Green jersey","createdBy":"bvittek","pinned":false,"text":["Battling at the front and making the most of the intermediate sprint, Mads Pedersen (Lidl-Trek) pushed his lead back up to 32 points to Jasper Philipsen (Alpecin-Premier Tech): 477 points for the Dane, 445 for the Belgian sprinter. Biniam Girmay (NSN) is still third with 361 points.
Today’s stage offers up to 45 points: 25 for the first rider at the intermediate sprint (Le Périer, km 89.4) and 20 for the stage winner."],"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T14:03:15+02:00","id":1353141,"type":"liv","highlight":false,"year":2026,"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T14:03:15+02:00","stage":19,"_id":"0b019d884039bd6a214de1dec88f697d45aa0fe032d8c0d19e9bb20c389ab399","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784894596096,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:green-jersey-52","model":"news"},{"picto":"liv_story","slug":"col-du-noyer-from-ocana-to-carapaz","locale":"en","createdAt":"2026-07-24T14:46:56+02:00","stageType":"HMG","id":1353386,"createdBy":"bvittek","title":"Col du Noyer: from Ocaña to Carapaz","updatedBy":"bvittek","text":["Tackled for the first time in 1970, Col du Noyer was the protagonist of a legendary feat the following year. At the bottom of the climb, 60 kilometres form the finish at Orcières-Merlette, Luis Ocaña distanced Lucien Van Impe, Joaquim Agostinho and Joop Zoetemelk, while Eddy Merckx was already trailing by a couple of minutes. The Spaniard flew to victory and opened massive gaps: 5’52’’ to Van Impe, second on the day, and 8’42’’ to Merckx, who fought back to the third place. Ocaña looked set to dethrone Merckx, the winner of the 1969 and 1970 editions, but he abandoned four days later after a crash on the downhill from Col de Menté.
Most recently, Col du Noyer featured in stage 17 of the Tour 2024. Richard Carapaz distanced his rivals on the climb and soloed to victory in Superdévoluy. The Ecuadorian climber is at the front again today, after his stage win at Orcières-Merlette yesterday."],"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T14:46:56+02:00","pinned":false,"year":2026,"type":"liv","highlight":false,"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T14:46:56+02:00","stage":19,"_id":"55ec141df8589b153ebb6a5eab8c05ed788955b861c5e2edcb263a004ac7c5bb","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784897216677,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:col-du-noyer-from-ocana-to-carapaz","model":"news"},{"videoLangs":[{"locale":"fr","title":"Découvrez le parcours de l'Étape 19"},{"title":"Discover the route of Stage 19","locale":"en"},{"locale":"de","title":"Discover the route of Etappe 19"},{"title":"Descubra el recorrido de la Etapa 19","locale":"es"}],"id":11514,"createdAt":"2026-07-24T08:15:00+02:00","isData":false,"isClub":false,"onlyClubTab":false,"url":"https://geo.dailymotion.com/player/xbbut.html?video=x9sikn0","onlyDataTab":false,"name":"Découvrez le parcours de l'Étape 19","_id":"83cbe3b2a4e6aca43f6c157600b46b91f7349b6caada92669daf6a19e5e11d4c","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"video-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784875478877,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"video:Découvrez le parcours de l'Étape 19","model":"video","title":"Discover the route of Stage 19","locale":"en"},{"type":"liv","highlight":false,"year":2026,"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T14:42:01+02:00","stage":19,"createdAt":"2026-07-24T14:42:01+02:00","stageType":"HMG","picto":"liv_group","locale":"en","slug":"the-leaders","title":"The 42 leaders","createdBy":"bvittek","updatedBy":"bvittek","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T14:45:41+02:00","text":["UAE Emirates RXG: Yates

The riders first rolled through Gap in the early morning of 16 July 1905, on their way from Grenoble to Toulon, where Hippolyte Aucouturier took the fourth of his five Tour stage wins.
The first time the race stopped in Gap was in 1931. Jeff Demuysere won stage 15, which started in Nice. Then Charles Pélissier conquered Grenoble, after 102 kilometres of racing from Gap.Today marks the 26th stage start in Gap, the first since 2024, when Victor Campenaerts escaped to victory in Barcelonnette. The town often gave way to stages in the Alps, including three Gap - Alpe d’Huez stages in 1991, 2006, and 2013."],"createdBy":"bvittek","title":"Gap, a historic venue of the Tour","updatedBy":"bvittek","_id":"5c29dd16f28123adcc6e606aff3d870b2e141327dd2fd4dfe4722d846431ee86","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784893047421,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:gap-a-historic-venue-of-the-tour","model":"news"},{"socialContentLangs":[{"createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","createdBy":"ganz","updatedBy":"ganz","locale":"fr","title":"Maillot blanc"},{"createdBy":"ganz","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","title":"White jersey","locale":"en","updatedBy":"ganz"},{"locale":"de","updatedBy":"ganz","title":"Weißes Trikot","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","createdBy":"ganz"}],"stage":{"timezone":"Europe/Paris","showGroups":true,"length":127.9,"podiumDisplayMode":"instantpodium","lengthDisplay":128,"stage":19,"date":"2026-07-24T00:00:00+02:00","type":"HMG","isCancelled":false},"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","highlight":false,"createdBy":"ganz","onlyDataTab":false,"onlyClubTab":false,"url":"https://www.instagram.com/p/DbK2dG-ObUh/?utm_source=ig_web_copy_link&igsh=MzRlODBiNWFlZA==","createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","id":8590,"pinned":false,"updatedBy":"ganz","embedCode":"

However, the Danish, who is highly determined to win the green jersey, should manage to limit the damage with the help of his Lidl-Trek teammates. Paul ! While Alpe d’Huez is hosting the finishes for stages 19 and 20 this year, this is not an unprecedented occurrence. To find two consecutive finishes at the same place, we only need to look back to the start of this Tour de France, when stages 1 and 2 concluded near the Barcelona’s Olympic Stadium. Alpe d’Huez itself had already hosted two back-to-back finishes via the famous 21-hairpins route in 1979, Joaquim Agostinho winning on the seventeenth day before Joop Zoetemelk the very next day.","publicationAt":"2026-07-24T13:13:00+00:00","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","updatedAt":null},"text":"Is it unprecedented to see two consecutive stages finish in the same place ?","status":"pending"},"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T13:13:00+00:00","locale":"en","widget":"involve","_id":"question-6a6364f4d780d4eaaa077168","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"interactiveContent-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784898829163,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"interactiveContent:enundefined-undefined","model":"interactive","isClub":false},{"highlight":false,"type":"liv","year":2026,"stage":19,"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T13:54:09+02:00","stageType":"HMG","createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:54:09+02:00","locale":"en","slug":"beautiful-weather","picto":"liv_sun","pinned":false,"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:54:09+02:00","text":["The sun is shining down on the 162 riders expected in Gap, where the temperature is 30°C. Fine weather is set to prevail throughout today’s stage, with temperatures cooling as the peloton gains altitude. Temperatures of 23°C are expected at the finish at Alpe d’Huez. A wind of 10 km/h, as forecast by Météo France, will be blowing eastwards."],"updatedBy":"bvittek","createdBy":"bvittek","title":"Beautiful weather","id":1353103,"_id":"6bd8f02e089ffcc0c7e8a7fcf3d2d14136142a72ad62b74724194ca7836844a3","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784894050036,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:beautiful-weather","model":"news"},{"title":"Claiming the polka-dot jersey at the eleventh hour?","createdBy":"bvittek","updatedBy":"bvittek","text":["Tadej Pogacar (UAE Emirates XRG) has been leading the mountains classification for 13 stages, since his stage win at Gavarnie-Gèdre past the Col du Tourmalet. Will the Slovenian star claim another polka-dot jersey on the Champs-Élysées, as he did in 2020, 2021, and 2025?
Valentin Paret-Peintre (Soudal Quick-Step) never gave up on the target he had set for himself before the Tour de France and, after the early ascents of Col Bayard and Col du Noyer, he is up to 84 points, while Pogacar has 70. And Richard Carapaz (EF Education-EasyPost) is a close rival: 74 points.
Since the polka-dot jersey was introduced in 1975, the winner of the KOM standings took it in the last three days of racing on six occasions. Giancarlo Bellini was the first to do so, in 1976, edging Lucien Van Impe on day 21 (penultimate stage). Then it happened five times between 2011 and 2024.
In 2020, Tadej Pogacar took the polka-dot jersey from Richard Carapaz on stage 20, while the Ecuadorian took his revenge on day 19 of the Tour 2024.
This year, everything should be decided on stage 20, with a whooping 70 KOM points on offer between Le Bourg d'Oisans and Alpe d'Huez."],"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T15:12:23+02:00","pinned":false,"id":1353471,"createdAt":"2026-07-24T15:12:23+02:00","stageType":"HMG","picto":"liv_polka_dot_jersey","locale":"en","slug":"claiming-the-polka-dot-jersey-at-the-eleventh-hour","publicationAt":"2026-07-24T15:12:23+02:00","stage":19,"type":"liv","highlight":true,"year":2026,"_id":"73d840fe3d7e20ea7e5a3a8c06d5f23f5630a0c0aeec437f3c1d94f12a13dc4c","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784898743906,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:claiming-the-polka-dot-jersey-at-the-eleventh-hour","model":"news"},{"stageType":"HMG","createdAt":"2026-07-24T14:15:34+02:00","slug":"stage-19-of-the-tour-de-france-2026-is-on","locale":"en","picto":"liv_actual_start","text":["The 162-man peloton just passed km 0.

It’s hard to say at this stage of the race. With the climbs of the Col Bayard and Col du Noyer right at the start, the day’s opening sets the scene for a breakaway to form, one that plenty of climbers could slip into. Given the stage's short distance, a win for the breakaway is entirely possible, indeed in recent years, it hasn't been uncommon to see the breakaway group fight for the victory in this ski resort. As for Tadej Pogacar, a win at Alpe d’Huez, via the famous 21-hairpin route, would be a prestigious achievement. However, it will be difficult for the Yellow Jersey to fully control a breakaway with his team, especially since riders like Tim Wellens and Florian Vermeersch have been struggling in recent days, on top of Brandon McNulty’s withdrawal yesterday. Even so, the battle between the breakaway and the peloton promises to be a hard-fought contest.","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","publicationAt":"2026-07-24T12:57:00+00:00","updatedAt":null},"context":{"stage":19,"category":null,"event":"tdf","locale":"en","name":"tdf","millesime":2026},"updatedBy":null,"_id":"6a636148096f7824b00d555b","createdAt":"2026-07-24T12:57:44+00:00","updatedAt":null,"createdBy":"Mouchon08","event":{"host":"https://www.letour.fr/","bubble":"cycling","trigram":"tdf","name":"Le Tour de France"}},"_id":"question-6a636148096f7824b00d555b","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"interactiveContent-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784897880597,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"interactiveContent:enundefined-undefined","model":"interactive","isClub":false},{"updatedBy":"bvittek","title":"A myth to begin with","createdBy":"bvittek","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T14:11:24+02:00","text":["Alpe d’Huez, which will be at the heart of stages 19 and 20, is a legendary climb that is hard to take your eyes off. But there are many more challenges to be faced in these two decisive days, including some ascents that made Tour history much earlier than the 21-hairpin climb.
In 1905, Col Bayard was the first Alpine pass on the route, during stage 4 (Grenoble > Toulon, 348 km). Julien Maitron was first at the summit.
Col Bayard featured in every edition between 1905 and 1921, and has been regularly used since. Today marks the 29th ascent in the history of the Tour.
On the most recent occasion, stage 17 of the Tour 2024, Magnus Cort was first at the summit. Richard Carapaz then led over Col du Noyer and took victory at Superdévoluy."],"pinned":false,"id":1353207,"createdAt":"2026-07-24T14:11:24+02:00","stageType":"HMG","picto":"liv_story","slug":"a-myth-to-begin-with","locale":"en","publicationAt":"2026-07-24T14:11:24+02:00","stage":19,"type":"liv","highlight":false,"year":2026,"_id":"9458d94ac5d16c44eb18922d1a83188a39291bdf7d111403e5c63ed6308bddcc","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784895084432,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:a-myth-to-begin-with","model":"news"},{"onlyClubTab":false,"url":"https://x.com/LeTour/status/2080636271261524387","socialContentLangs":[{"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T14:51:49+02:00","updatedBy":"ganz","createdBy":"ganz","id":21085,"locale":"fr","createdAt":"2026-07-24T14:51:05+02:00","title":"Les July: Page and Kübler","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:45:14+02:00","pinned":false,"text":["Born on 24 July 2001 in Chartres, Hugo Page is celebrating his 25th birthday on the route of the Tour de France 2026! This is his third consecutive appearance in the race, and he recently achieved his best result to date by finishing 12th in Voiron (stage 17). Hugo Page has one professional victory to his name, secured in a sprint finish on the Tour du Limousin-Périgord – Nouvelle-Aquitaine.
Ferdinand Kübler (1919–2016) was born on 24 July in Marthalen, Switzerland. Turning professional in 1940, he had to wait until the end of the Second World War to demonstrate the full extent of his abilities, which would lead him to become the first Swiss winner of the Tour de France in 1950. He also won eight stages and the points classification (1954), was world road champion (1951), a two-time winner of Liège–Bastogne–Liège (1951–52) and La Flèche Wallonne (also 1951–52), 'Ferdi' is one of the greatest Swiss cyclists in history."],"id":1353087,"_id":"4f30a2c3775dfae79895d20878c58bbb486264d0743dbf49563cf232e872cb84","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784893515137,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:24-july-page-and-kubler","model":"news"},{"url":"https://x.com/LeTour/status/2080625324912517410","id":8594,"createdAt":"2026-07-24T14:08:04+02:00","pinned":false,"updatedBy":"ganz","type":"twitter","publicationAt":"2026-07-24T14:08:04+02:00","socialContentLangs":[{"updatedBy":"ganz","locale":"fr","title":"Vers It will be reached after the legendary 13.8 km ascent, with its 21 hairpins and its average gradient of 8.1%.
The peloton are set to roll from Gap at 14:00, to reach km 0 at 14:15. The winners will be crowned at around 17:30."],"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:30:49+02:00","updatedBy":"bvittek","title":"Welcome to the live coverage of stage 19 of the Tour de France 2026","createdBy":"bvittek","id":1353061,"_id":"3083073ecf289dd1f921c5a4af6f002bf3327a37d3539454a40f6f80779dbbf2","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784892650125,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:welcome-to-the-live-coverage-of-stage-19-of-the-tour-de-france-2026","model":"news"},{"createdAt":"2026-07-24T14:03:15+02:00","stageType":"HMG","picto":"liv_green_jersey","slug":"green-jersey-52","locale":"en","updatedBy":"bvittek","title":"Green jersey","createdBy":"bvittek","pinned":false,"text":["Battling at the front and making the most of the intermediate sprint, Mads Pedersen (Lidl-Trek) pushed his lead back up to 32 points to Jasper Philipsen (Alpecin-Premier Tech): 477 points for the Dane, 445 for the Belgian sprinter. Biniam Girmay (NSN) is still third with 361 points.
Today’s stage offers up to 45 points: 25 for the first rider at the intermediate sprint (Le Périer, km 89.4) and 20 for the stage winner."],"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T14:03:15+02:00","id":1353141,"type":"liv","highlight":false,"year":2026,"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T14:03:15+02:00","stage":19,"_id":"0b019d884039bd6a214de1dec88f697d45aa0fe032d8c0d19e9bb20c389ab399","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784894596096,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:green-jersey-52","model":"news"},{"picto":"liv_story","slug":"col-du-noyer-from-ocana-to-carapaz","locale":"en","createdAt":"2026-07-24T14:46:56+02:00","stageType":"HMG","id":1353386,"createdBy":"bvittek","title":"Col du Noyer: from Ocaña to Carapaz","updatedBy":"bvittek","text":["Tackled for the first time in 1970, Col du Noyer was the protagonist of a legendary feat the following year. At the bottom of the climb, 60 kilometres form the finish at Orcières-Merlette, Luis Ocaña distanced Lucien Van Impe, Joaquim Agostinho and Joop Zoetemelk, while Eddy Merckx was already trailing by a couple of minutes. The Spaniard flew to victory and opened massive gaps: 5’52’’ to Van Impe, second on the day, and 8’42’’ to Merckx, who fought back to the third place. Ocaña looked set to dethrone Merckx, the winner of the 1969 and 1970 editions, but he abandoned four days later after a crash on the downhill from Col de Menté.
Most recently, Col du Noyer featured in stage 17 of the Tour 2024. Richard Carapaz distanced his rivals on the climb and soloed to victory in Superdévoluy. The Ecuadorian climber is at the front again today, after his stage win at Orcières-Merlette yesterday."],"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T14:46:56+02:00","pinned":false,"year":2026,"type":"liv","highlight":false,"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T14:46:56+02:00","stage":19,"_id":"55ec141df8589b153ebb6a5eab8c05ed788955b861c5e2edcb263a004ac7c5bb","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784897216677,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:col-du-noyer-from-ocana-to-carapaz","model":"news"},{"videoLangs":[{"locale":"fr","title":"Découvrez le parcours de l'Étape 19"},{"title":"Discover the route of Stage 19","locale":"en"},{"locale":"de","title":"Discover the route of Etappe 19"},{"title":"Descubra el recorrido de la Etapa 19","locale":"es"}],"id":11514,"createdAt":"2026-07-24T08:15:00+02:00","isData":false,"isClub":false,"onlyClubTab":false,"url":"https://geo.dailymotion.com/player/xbbut.html?video=x9sikn0","onlyDataTab":false,"name":"Découvrez le parcours de l'Étape 19","_id":"83cbe3b2a4e6aca43f6c157600b46b91f7349b6caada92669daf6a19e5e11d4c","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"video-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784875478877,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"video:Découvrez le parcours de l'Étape 19","model":"video","title":"Discover the route of Stage 19","locale":"en"},{"type":"liv","highlight":false,"year":2026,"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T14:42:01+02:00","stage":19,"createdAt":"2026-07-24T14:42:01+02:00","stageType":"HMG","picto":"liv_group","locale":"en","slug":"the-leaders","title":"The 42 leaders","createdBy":"bvittek","updatedBy":"bvittek","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T14:45:41+02:00","text":["UAE Emirates RXG: Yates

The riders first rolled through Gap in the early morning of 16 July 1905, on their way from Grenoble to Toulon, where Hippolyte Aucouturier took the fourth of his five Tour stage wins.
The first time the race stopped in Gap was in 1931. Jeff Demuysere won stage 15, which started in Nice. Then Charles Pélissier conquered Grenoble, after 102 kilometres of racing from Gap.Today marks the 26th stage start in Gap, the first since 2024, when Victor Campenaerts escaped to victory in Barcelonnette. The town often gave way to stages in the Alps, including three Gap - Alpe d’Huez stages in 1991, 2006, and 2013."],"createdBy":"bvittek","title":"Gap, a historic venue of the Tour","updatedBy":"bvittek","_id":"5c29dd16f28123adcc6e606aff3d870b2e141327dd2fd4dfe4722d846431ee86","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784893047421,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:gap-a-historic-venue-of-the-tour","model":"news"},{"socialContentLangs":[{"createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","createdBy":"ganz","updatedBy":"ganz","locale":"fr","title":"Maillot blanc"},{"createdBy":"ganz","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","title":"White jersey","locale":"en","updatedBy":"ganz"},{"locale":"de","updatedBy":"ganz","title":"Weißes Trikot","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","createdBy":"ganz"}],"stage":{"timezone":"Europe/Paris","showGroups":true,"length":127.9,"podiumDisplayMode":"instantpodium","lengthDisplay":128,"stage":19,"date":"2026-07-24T00:00:00+02:00","type":"HMG","isCancelled":false},"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","highlight":false,"createdBy":"ganz","onlyDataTab":false,"onlyClubTab":false,"url":"https://www.instagram.com/p/DbK2dG-ObUh/?utm_source=ig_web_copy_link&igsh=MzRlODBiNWFlZA==","createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","id":8590,"pinned":false,"updatedBy":"ganz","embedCode":"

While Alpe d’Huez is hosting the finishes for stages 19 and 20 this year, this is not an unprecedented occurrence. To find two consecutive finishes at the same place, we only need to look back to the start of this Tour de France, when stages 1 and 2 concluded near the Barcelona’s Olympic Stadium. Alpe d’Huez itself had already hosted two back-to-back finishes via the famous 21-hairpins route in 1979, Joaquim Agostinho winning on the seventeenth day before Joop Zoetemelk the very next day.","publicationAt":"2026-07-24T13:13:00+00:00","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","updatedAt":null},"text":"Is it unprecedented to see two consecutive stages finish in the same place ?","status":"pending"},"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T13:13:00+00:00","locale":"en","widget":"involve","_id":"question-6a6364f4d780d4eaaa077168","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"interactiveContent-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784898829163,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"interactiveContent:enundefined-undefined","model":"interactive","isClub":false},{"highlight":false,"type":"liv","year":2026,"stage":19,"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T13:54:09+02:00","stageType":"HMG","createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:54:09+02:00","locale":"en","slug":"beautiful-weather","picto":"liv_sun","pinned":false,"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:54:09+02:00","text":["The sun is shining down on the 162 riders expected in Gap, where the temperature is 30°C. A wind of 10 km/h, as forecast by Météo France, will be blowing eastwards."],"updatedBy":"bvittek","createdBy":"bvittek","title":"Beautiful weather","id":1353103,"_id":"6bd8f02e089ffcc0c7e8a7fcf3d2d14136142a72ad62b74724194ca7836844a3","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784894050036,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:beautiful-weather","model":"news"},{"title":"Claiming the polka-dot jersey at the eleventh hour?","createdBy":"bvittek","updatedBy":"bvittek","text":["Tadej Pogacar (UAE Emirates XRG) has been leading the mountains classification for 13 stages, since his stage win at Gavarnie-Gèdre past the Col du Tourmalet. Will the Slovenian star claim another polka-dot jersey on the Champs-Élysées, as he did in 2020, 2021, and 2025?
Valentin Paret-Peintre (Soudal Quick-Step) never gave up on the target he had set for himself before the Tour de France and, after the early ascents of Col Bayard and Col du Noyer, he is up to 84 points, while Pogacar has 70. And Richard Carapaz (EF Education-EasyPost) is a close rival: 74 points.
Since the polka-dot jersey was introduced in 1975, the winner of the KOM standings took it in the last three days of racing on six occasions. Giancarlo Bellini was the first to do so, in 1976, edging Lucien Van Impe on day 21 (penultimate stage). Then it happened five times between 2011 and 2024.
In 2020, Tadej Pogacar took the polka-dot jersey from Richard Carapaz on stage 20, while the Ecuadorian took his revenge on day 19 of the Tour 2024.
This year, everything should be decided on stage 20, with a whooping 70 KOM points on offer between Le Bourg d'Oisans and Alpe d'Huez."],"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T15:12:23+02:00","pinned":false,"id":1353471,"createdAt":"2026-07-24T15:12:23+02:00","stageType":"HMG","picto":"liv_polka_dot_jersey","locale":"en","slug":"claiming-the-polka-dot-jersey-at-the-eleventh-hour","publicationAt":"2026-07-24T15:12:23+02:00","stage":19,"type":"liv","highlight":true,"year":2026,"_id":"73d840fe3d7e20ea7e5a3a8c06d5f23f5630a0c0aeec437f3c1d94f12a13dc4c","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784898743906,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:claiming-the-polka-dot-jersey-at-the-eleventh-hour","model":"news"},{"stageType":"HMG","createdAt":"2026-07-24T14:15:34+02:00","slug":"stage-19-of-the-tour-de-france-2026-is-on","locale":"en","picto":"liv_actual_start","text":["The 162-man peloton just passed km 0.

It’s hard to say at this stage of the race. With the climbs of the Col Bayard and Col du Noyer right at the start, the day’s opening sets the scene for a breakaway to form, one that plenty of climbers could slip into. Given the stage's short distance, a win for the breakaway is entirely possible, indeed in recent years, it hasn't been uncommon to see the breakaway group fight for the victory in this ski resort. As for Tadej Pogacar, a win at Alpe d’Huez, via the famous 21-hairpin route, would be a prestigious achievement. However, it will be difficult for the Yellow Jersey to fully control a breakaway with his team, especially since riders like Tim Wellens and Florian Vermeersch have been struggling in recent days, on top of Brandon McNulty’s withdrawal yesterday. Even so, the battle between the breakaway and the peloton promises to be a hard-fought contest.","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","publicationAt":"2026-07-24T12:57:00+00:00","updatedAt":null},"context":{"stage":19,"category":null,"event":"tdf","locale":"en","name":"tdf","millesime":2026},"updatedBy":null,"_id":"6a636148096f7824b00d555b","createdAt":"2026-07-24T12:57:44+00:00","updatedAt":null,"createdBy":"Mouchon08","event":{"host":"https://www.letour.fr/","bubble":"cycling","trigram":"tdf","name":"Le Tour de France"}},"_id":"question-6a636148096f7824b00d555b","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"interactiveContent-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784897880597,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"interactiveContent:enundefined-undefined","model":"interactive","isClub":false},{"updatedBy":"bvittek","title":"A myth to begin with","createdBy":"bvittek","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T14:11:24+02:00","text":["Alpe d’Huez, which will be at the heart of stages 19 and 20, is a legendary climb that is hard to take your eyes off. But there are many more challenges to be faced in these two decisive days, including some ascents that made Tour history much earlier than the 21-hairpin climb.
In 1905, Col Bayard was the first Alpine pass on the route, during stage 4 (Grenoble > Toulon, 348 km). Julien Maitron was first at the summit.
Col Bayard featured in every edition between 1905 and 1921, and has been regularly used since. Today marks the 29th ascent in the history of the Tour.
On the most recent occasion, stage 17 of the Tour 2024, Magnus Cort was first at the summit. Richard Carapaz then led over Col du Noyer and took victory at Superdévoluy."],"pinned":false,"id":1353207,"createdAt":"2026-07-24T14:11:24+02:00","stageType":"HMG","picto":"liv_story","slug":"a-myth-to-begin-with","locale":"en","publicationAt":"2026-07-24T14:11:24+02:00","stage":19,"type":"liv","highlight":false,"year":2026,"_id":"9458d94ac5d16c44eb18922d1a83188a39291bdf7d111403e5c63ed6308bddcc","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784895084432,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:a-myth-to-begin-with","model":"news"},{"onlyClubTab":false,"url":"https://x.com/LeTour/status/2080636271261524387","socialContentLangs":[{"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T14:51:49+02:00","updatedBy":"ganz","createdBy":"ganz","id":21085,"locale":"fr","createdAt":"2026-07-24T14:51:05+02:00","title":"Les July: Page and Kübler","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:45:14+02:00","pinned":false,"text":["Born on 24 July 2001 in Chartres, Hugo Page is celebrating his 25th birthday on the route of the Tour de France 2026! This is his third consecutive appearance in the race, and he recently achieved his best result to date by finishing 12th in Voiron (stage 17). Hugo Page has one professional victory to his name, secured in a sprint finish on the Tour du Limousin-Périgord – Nouvelle-Aquitaine.
Ferdinand Kübler (1919–2016) was born on 24 July in Marthalen, Switzerland. Turning professional in 1940, he had to wait until the end of the Second World War to demonstrate the full extent of his abilities, which would lead him to become the first Swiss winner of the Tour de France in 1950. He also won eight stages and the points classification (1954), was world road champion (1951), a two-time winner of Liège–Bastogne–Liège (1951–52) and La Flèche Wallonne (also 1951–52), 'Ferdi' is one of the greatest Swiss cyclists in After Orcières-Merlette, the peloton head to another iconic venue: the first mountain top finish in the history of the Tour, and the one that has most often featured! It will be reached after the legendary 13.8 km ascent, with its 21 hairpins and its average gradient of 8.1%.
The peloton are set to roll from Gap at 14:00, to reach km 0 at 14:15. The winners will be crowned at around 17:30."],"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:30:49+02:00","updatedBy":"bvittek","title":"Welcome to the live coverage of stage 19 of the Tour de France 2026","createdBy":"bvittek","id":1353061,"_id":"3083073ecf289dd1f921c5a4af6f002bf3327a37d3539454a40f6f80779dbbf2","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784892650125,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:welcome-to-the-live-coverage-of-stage-19-of-the-tour-de-france-2026","model":"news"},{"createdAt":"2026-07-24T14:03:15+02:00","stageType":"HMG","picto":"liv_green_jersey","slug":"green-jersey-52","locale":"en","updatedBy":"bvittek","title":"Green jersey","createdBy":"bvittek","pinned":false,"text":["Battling at the front and making the most of the intermediate sprint, Mads Pedersen (Lidl-Trek) pushed his lead back up to 32 points to Jasper Philipsen (Alpecin-Premier Tech): 477 points for the Dane, 445 for the Belgian sprinter. Biniam Girmay (NSN) is still third with 361 points.
Today’s stage offers up to 45 points: 25 for the first rider at the intermediate sprint (Le Périer, km 89.4) and 20 for the stage winner."],"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T14:03:15+02:00","id":1353141,"type":"liv","highlight":false,"year":2026,"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T14:03:15+02:00","stage":19,"_id":"0b019d884039bd6a214de1dec88f697d45aa0fe032d8c0d19e9bb20c389ab399","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784894596096,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:green-jersey-52","model":"news"},{"picto":"liv_story","slug":"col-du-noyer-from-ocana-to-carapaz","locale":"en","createdAt":"2026-07-24T14:46:56+02:00","stageType":"HMG","id":1353386,"createdBy":"bvittek","title":"Col du Noyer: from Ocaña to Carapaz","updatedBy":"bvittek","text":["Tackled for the first time in 1970, Col du Noyer was the protagonist of a legendary feat the following year. At the bottom of the climb, 60 kilometres form the finish at Orcières-Merlette, Luis Ocaña distanced Lucien Van Impe, Joaquim Agostinho and Joop Zoetemelk, while Eddy Merckx was already trailing by a couple of minutes. The Spaniard flew to victory and opened massive gaps: 5’52’’ to Van Impe, second on the day, and 8’42’’ to Merckx, who fought back to the third place. Ocaña looked set to dethrone Merckx, the winner of the 1969 and 1970 editions, but he abandoned four days later after a crash on the downhill from Col de Menté.
Most recently, Col du Noyer featured in stage 17 of the Tour 2024. Richard Carapaz distanced his rivals on the climb and soloed to victory in Superdévoluy. The Ecuadorian climber is at the front again today, after his stage win at Orcières-Merlette yesterday."],"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T14:46:56+02:00","pinned":false,"year":2026,"type":"liv","highlight":false,"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T14:46:56+02:00","stage":19,"_id":"55ec141df8589b153ebb6a5eab8c05ed788955b861c5e2edcb263a004ac7c5bb","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784897216677,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:col-du-noyer-from-ocana-to-carapaz","model":"news"},{"videoLangs":[{"locale":"fr","title":"Découvrez le parcours de l'Étape 19"},{"title":"Discover the route of Stage 19","locale":"en"},{"locale":"de","title":"Discover the route of Etappe 19"},{"title":"Descubra el recorrido de la Etapa 19","locale":"es"}],"id":11514,"createdAt":"2026-07-24T08:15:00+02:00","isData":false,"isClub":false,"onlyClubTab":false,"url":"https://geo.dailymotion.com/player/xbbut.html?video=x9sikn0","onlyDataTab":false,"name":"Découvrez le parcours de l'Étape 19","_id":"83cbe3b2a4e6aca43f6c157600b46b91f7349b6caada92669daf6a19e5e11d4c","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"video-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784875478877,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"video:Découvrez le parcours de l'Étape 19","model":"video","title":"Discover the route of Stage 19","locale":"en"},{"type":"liv","highlight":false,"year":2026,"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T14:42:01+02:00","stage":19,"createdAt":"2026-07-24T14:42:01+02:00","stageType":"HMG","picto":"liv_group","locale":"en","slug":"the-leaders","title":"The 42 leaders","createdBy":"bvittek","updatedBy":"bvittek","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T14:45:41+02:00","text":["UAE Emirates RXG: Yates

The riders first rolled through Gap in the early morning of 16 July 1905, on their way from Grenoble to Toulon, where Hippolyte Aucouturier took the fourth of his five Tour stage wins.
The first time the race stopped in Gap was in 1931. Jeff Demuysere won stage 15, which started in Nice. Then Charles Pélissier conquered Grenoble, after 102 kilometres of racing from Gap.Today marks the 26th stage start in Gap, the first since 2024, when Victor Campenaerts escaped to victory in Barcelonnette. The town often gave way to stages in the Alps, including three Gap - Alpe d’Huez stages in 1991, 2006, and 2013."],"createdBy":"bvittek","title":"Gap, a historic venue of the Tour","updatedBy":"bvittek","_id":"5c29dd16f28123adcc6e606aff3d870b2e141327dd2fd4dfe4722d846431ee86","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784893047421,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:gap-a-historic-venue-of-the-tour","model":"news"},{"socialContentLangs":[{"createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","createdBy":"ganz","updatedBy":"ganz","locale":"fr","title":"Maillot blanc"},{"createdBy":"ganz","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","title":"White jersey","locale":"en","updatedBy":"ganz"},{"locale":"de","updatedBy":"ganz","title":"Weißes Trikot","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","createdBy":"ganz"}],"stage":{"timezone":"Europe/Paris","showGroups":true,"length":127.9,"podiumDisplayMode":"instantpodium","lengthDisplay":128,"stage":19,"date":"2026-07-24T00:00:00+02:00","type":"HMG","isCancelled":false},"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","highlight":false,"createdBy":"ganz","onlyDataTab":false,"onlyClubTab":false,"url":"https://www.instagram.com/p/DbK2dG-ObUh/?utm_source=ig_web_copy_link&igsh=MzRlODBiNWFlZA==","createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","id":8590,"pinned":false,"updatedBy":"ganz","embedCode":"

Paul ! While Alpe d’Huez is hosting the finishes for stages 19 and 20 this year, this is not an unprecedented occurrence. To find two consecutive finishes at the same place, we only need to look back to the start of this Tour de France, when stages 1 and 2 concluded near the Barcelona’s Olympic Stadium. Alpe d’Huez itself had already hosted two back-to-back finishes via the famous 21-hairpins route in 1979, Joaquim Agostinho winning on the seventeenth day before Joop Zoetemelk the very next day.","publicationAt":"2026-07-24T13:13:00+00:00","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","updatedAt":null},"text":"Is it unprecedented to see two consecutive stages finish in the same place ?","status":"pending"},"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T13:13:00+00:00","locale":"en","widget":"involve","_id":"question-6a6364f4d780d4eaaa077168","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"interactiveContent-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784898829163,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"interactiveContent:enundefined-undefined","model":"interactive","isClub":false},{"highlight":false,"type":"liv","year":2026,"stage":19,"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T13:54:09+02:00","stageType":"HMG","createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:54:09+02:00","locale":"en","slug":"beautiful-weather","picto":"liv_sun","pinned":false,"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:54:09+02:00","text":["The sun is shining down on the 162 riders expected in Gap, where the temperature is 30°C. Fine weather is set to prevail throughout today’s stage, with temperatures cooling as the peloton gains altitude. Temperatures of 23°C are expected at the finish at Alpe d’Huez. A wind of 10 km/h, as forecast by Météo France, will be blowing eastwards."],"updatedBy":"bvittek","createdBy":"bvittek","title":"Beautiful weather","id":1353103,"_id":"6bd8f02e089ffcc0c7e8a7fcf3d2d14136142a72ad62b74724194ca7836844a3","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784894050036,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:beautiful-weather","model":"news"},{"title":"Claiming the polka-dot jersey at the eleventh hour?","createdBy":"bvittek","updatedBy":"bvittek","text":["Tadej Pogacar (UAE Emirates XRG) has been leading the mountains classification for 13 stages, since his stage win at Gavarnie-Gèdre past the Col du Tourmalet. Will the Slovenian star claim another polka-dot jersey on the Champs-Élysées, as he did in 2020, 2021, and 2025?
Valentin Paret-Peintre (Soudal Quick-Step) never gave up on the target he had set for himself before the Tour de France and, after the early ascents of Col Bayard and Col du Noyer, he is up to 84 points, while Pogacar has 70. And Richard Carapaz (EF Education-EasyPost) is a close rival: 74 points.
Since the polka-dot jersey was introduced in 1975, the winner of the KOM standings took it in the last three days of racing on six occasions. Giancarlo Bellini was the first to do so, in 1976, edging Lucien Van Impe on day 21 (penultimate stage). Then it happened five times between 2011 and 2024.
In 2020, Tadej Pogacar took the polka-dot jersey from Richard Carapaz on stage 20, while the Ecuadorian took his revenge on day 19 of the Tour 2024.
This year, everything should be decided on stage 20, with a whooping 70 KOM points on offer between Le Bourg d'Oisans and Alpe d'Huez."],"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T15:12:23+02:00","pinned":false,"id":1353471,"createdAt":"2026-07-24T15:12:23+02:00","stageType":"HMG","picto":"liv_polka_dot_jersey","locale":"en","slug":"claiming-the-polka-dot-jersey-at-the-eleventh-hour","publicationAt":"2026-07-24T15:12:23+02:00","stage":19,"type":"liv","highlight":true,"year":2026,"_id":"73d840fe3d7e20ea7e5a3a8c06d5f23f5630a0c0aeec437f3c1d94f12a13dc4c","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784898743906,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:claiming-the-polka-dot-jersey-at-the-eleventh-hour","model":"news"},{"stageType":"HMG","createdAt":"2026-07-24T14:15:34+02:00","slug":"stage-19-of-the-tour-de-france-2026-is-on","locale":"en","picto":"liv_actual_start","text":["The 162-man peloton just passed km 0.

With the climbs of the Col Bayard and Col du Noyer right at the start, the day’s opening sets the scene for a breakaway to form, one that plenty of climbers could slip into. Given the stage's short distance, a win for the breakaway is entirely possible, indeed in recent years, it hasn't been uncommon to see the breakaway group fight for the victory in this ski resort. As for Tadej Pogacar, a win at Alpe d’Huez, via the famous 21-hairpin route, would be a prestigious achievement. However, it will be difficult for the Yellow Jersey to fully control a breakaway with his team, especially since riders like Tim Wellens and Florian Vermeersch have been struggling in recent days, on top of Brandon McNulty’s withdrawal yesterday. Even so, the battle between the breakaway and the peloton promises to be a hard-fought contest.","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","publicationAt":"2026-07-24T12:57:00+00:00","updatedAt":null},"context":{"stage":19,"category":null,"event":"tdf","locale":"en","name":"tdf","millesime":2026},"updatedBy":null,"_id":"6a636148096f7824b00d555b","createdAt":"2026-07-24T12:57:44+00:00","updatedAt":null,"createdBy":"Mouchon08","event":{"host":"https://www.letour.fr/","bubble":"cycling","trigram":"tdf","name":"Le Tour de France"}},"_id":"question-6a636148096f7824b00d555b","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"interactiveContent-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784897880597,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"interactiveContent:enundefined-undefined","model":"interactive","isClub":false},{"updatedBy":"bvittek","title":"A myth to begin with","createdBy":"bvittek","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T14:11:24+02:00","text":["Alpe d’Huez, which will be at the heart of stages 19 and 20, is a legendary climb that is hard to take your eyes off. But there are many more challenges to be faced in these two decisive days, including some ascents that made Tour history much earlier than the 21-hairpin climb.
In 1905, Col Bayard was the first Alpine pass on the route, during stage 4 (Grenoble > Toulon, 348 km). Julien Maitron was first at the summit.
Col Bayard featured in every edition between 1905 and 1921, and has been regularly used since. Today marks the 29th ascent in the history of the Tour.
On the most recent occasion, stage 17 of the Tour 2024, Magnus Cort was first at the summit. Richard Carapaz then led over Col du Noyer and took victory at Superdévoluy."],"pinned":false,"id":1353207,"createdAt":"2026-07-24T14:11:24+02:00","stageType":"HMG","picto":"liv_story","slug":"a-myth-to-begin-with","locale":"en","publicationAt":"2026-07-24T14:11:24+02:00","stage":19,"type":"liv","highlight":false,"year":2026,"_id":"9458d94ac5d16c44eb18922d1a83188a39291bdf7d111403e5c63ed6308bddcc","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784895084432,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:a-myth-to-begin-with","model":"news"},{"onlyClubTab":false,"url":"https://x.com/LeTour/status/2080636271261524387","socialContentLangs":[{"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T14:51:49+02:00","updatedBy":"ganz","createdBy":"ganz","id":21085,"locale":"fr","createdAt":"2026-07-24T14:51:05+02:00","title":"Les July: Page and Kübler","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:45:14+02:00","pinned":false,"text":["Born on 24 July 2001 in Chartres, Hugo Page is celebrating his 25th birthday on the route of the Tour de France 2026! This is his third consecutive appearance in the race, and he recently achieved his best result to date by finishing 12th in Voiron (stage 17). Hugo Page has one professional victory to his name, secured in a sprint finish on the Tour du Limousin-Périgord – Nouvelle-Aquitaine.
Ferdinand Kübler (1919–2016) was born on 24 July in Marthalen, Switzerland. Turning professional in 1940, he had to wait until the end of the Second World War to demonstrate the full extent of his abilities, which would lead him to become the first Swiss winner of the Tour de France in 1950. He also won eight stages and the points classification (1954), was world road champion (1951), a two-time winner of Liège–Bastogne–Liège (1951–52) and La Flèche Wallonne (also 1951–52), 'Ferdi' is one of the greatest Swiss cyclists in The peloton are set to roll from Gap at 14:00, to reach km 0 at 14:15. Biniam Girmay (NSN) is still third with 361 points.
Today’s stage offers up to 45 points: 25 for the first rider at the intermediate sprint (Le Périer, km 89.4) and 20 for the stage winner."],"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T14:03:15+02:00","id":1353141,"type":"liv","highlight":false,"year":2026,"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T14:03:15+02:00","stage":19,"_id":"0b019d884039bd6a214de1dec88f697d45aa0fe032d8c0d19e9bb20c389ab399","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784894596096,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:green-jersey-52","model":"news"},{"picto":"liv_story","slug":"col-du-noyer-from-ocana-to-carapaz","locale":"en","createdAt":"2026-07-24T14:46:56+02:00","stageType":"HMG","id":1353386,"createdBy":"bvittek","title":"Col du Noyer: from Ocaña to Carapaz","updatedBy":"bvittek","text":["Tackled for the first time in 1970, Col du Noyer was the protagonist of a legendary feat the following year. At the bottom of the climb, 60 kilometres form the finish at Orcières-Merlette, Luis Ocaña distanced Lucien Van Impe, Joaquim Agostinho and Joop Zoetemelk, while Eddy Merckx was already trailing by a couple of minutes. The Spaniard flew to victory and opened massive gaps: 5’52’’ to Van Impe, second on the day, and 8’42’’ to Merckx, who fought back to the third place. Ocaña looked set to dethrone Merckx, the winner of the 1969 and 1970 editions, but he abandoned four days later after a crash on the downhill from Col de Menté.
Most recently, Col du Noyer featured in stage 17 of the Tour 2024. Richard Carapaz distanced his rivals on the climb and soloed to victory in Superdévoluy. The Ecuadorian climber is at the front again today, after his stage win at Orcières-Merlette yesterday."],"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T14:46:56+02:00","pinned":false,"year":2026,"type":"liv","highlight":false,"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T14:46:56+02:00","stage":19,"_id":"55ec141df8589b153ebb6a5eab8c05ed788955b861c5e2edcb263a004ac7c5bb","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784897216677,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:col-du-noyer-from-ocana-to-carapaz","model":"news"},{"videoLangs":[{"locale":"fr","title":"Découvrez le parcours de l'Étape 19"},{"title":"Discover the route of Stage 19","locale":"en"},{"locale":"de","title":"Discover the route of Etappe 19"},{"title":"Descubra el recorrido de la Etapa 19","locale":"es"}],"id":11514,"createdAt":"2026-07-24T08:15:00+02:00","isData":false,"isClub":false,"onlyClubTab":false,"url":"https://geo.dailymotion.com/player/xbbut.html?video=x9sikn0","onlyDataTab":false,"name":"Découvrez le parcours de l'Étape 19","_id":"83cbe3b2a4e6aca43f6c157600b46b91f7349b6caada92669daf6a19e5e11d4c","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"video-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784875478877,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"video:Découvrez le parcours de l'Étape 19","model":"video","title":"Discover the route of Stage 19","locale":"en"},{"type":"liv","highlight":false,"year":2026,"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T14:42:01+02:00","stage":19,"createdAt":"2026-07-24T14:42:01+02:00","stageType":"HMG","picto":"liv_group","locale":"en","slug":"the-leaders","title":"The 42 leaders","createdBy":"bvittek","updatedBy":"bvittek","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T14:45:41+02:00","text":["UAE Emirates RXG: Yates

The riders first rolled through Gap in the early morning of 16 July 1905, on their way from Grenoble to Toulon, where Hippolyte Aucouturier took the fourth of his five Tour stage wins.
The first time the race stopped in Gap was in 1931. Jeff Demuysere won stage 15, which started in Nice. Then Charles Pélissier conquered Grenoble, after 102 kilometres of racing from Gap.Today marks the 26th stage start in Gap, the first since 2024, when Victor Campenaerts escaped to victory in Barcelonnette. The town often gave way to stages in the Alps, including three Gap - Alpe d’Huez stages in 1991, 2006, and 2013."],"createdBy":"bvittek","title":"Gap, a historic venue of the Tour","updatedBy":"bvittek","_id":"5c29dd16f28123adcc6e606aff3d870b2e141327dd2fd4dfe4722d846431ee86","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784893047421,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:gap-a-historic-venue-of-the-tour","model":"news"},{"socialContentLangs":[{"createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","createdBy":"ganz","updatedBy":"ganz","locale":"fr","title":"Maillot blanc"},{"createdBy":"ganz","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","title":"White jersey","locale":"en","updatedBy":"ganz"},{"locale":"de","updatedBy":"ganz","title":"Weißes Trikot","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","createdBy":"ganz"}],"stage":{"timezone":"Europe/Paris","showGroups":true,"length":127.9,"podiumDisplayMode":"instantpodium","lengthDisplay":128,"stage":19,"date":"2026-07-24T00:00:00+02:00","type":"HMG","isCancelled":false},"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","highlight":false,"createdBy":"ganz","onlyDataTab":false,"onlyClubTab":false,"url":"https://www.instagram.com/p/DbK2dG-ObUh/?utm_source=ig_web_copy_link&igsh=MzRlODBiNWFlZA==","createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","id":8590,"pinned":false,"updatedBy":"ganz","embedCode":"

While Alpe d’Huez is hosting the finishes for stages 19 and 20 this year, this is not an unprecedented occurrence. To find two consecutive finishes at the same place, we only need to look back to the start of this Tour de France, when stages 1 and 2 concluded near the Barcelona’s Olympic Stadium. Alpe d’Huez itself had already hosted two back-to-back finishes via the famous 21-hairpins route in 1979, Joaquim Agostinho winning on the seventeenth day before Joop Zoetemelk the very next day.","publicationAt":"2026-07-24T13:13:00+00:00","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","updatedAt":null},"text":"Is it unprecedented to see two consecutive stages finish in the same place ?","status":"pending"},"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T13:13:00+00:00","locale":"en","widget":"involve","_id":"question-6a6364f4d780d4eaaa077168","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"interactiveContent-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784898829163,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"interactiveContent:enundefined-undefined","model":"interactive","isClub":false},{"highlight":false,"type":"liv","year":2026,"stage":19,"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T13:54:09+02:00","stageType":"HMG","createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:54:09+02:00","locale":"en","slug":"beautiful-weather","picto":"liv_sun","pinned":false,"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:54:09+02:00","text":["The sun is shining down on the 162 riders expected in Gap, where the temperature is 30°C. Will the Slovenian star claim another polka-dot jersey on the Champs-Élysées, as he did in 2020, 2021, and 2025?
Valentin Paret-Peintre (Soudal Quick-Step) never gave up on the target he had set for himself before the Tour de France and, after the early ascents of Col Bayard and Col du Noyer, he is up to 84 points, while Pogacar has 70. And Richard Carapaz (EF Education-EasyPost) is a close rival: 74 points.
Since the polka-dot jersey was introduced in 1975, the winner of the KOM standings took it in the last three days of racing on six occasions. Giancarlo Bellini was the first to do so, in 1976, edging Lucien Van Impe on day 21 (penultimate stage). Then it happened five times between 2011 and 2024.
In 2020, Tadej Pogacar took the polka-dot jersey from Richard Carapaz on stage 20, while the Ecuadorian took his revenge on day 19 of the Tour 2024.
This year, everything should be decided on stage 20, with a whooping 70 KOM points on offer between Le Bourg d'Oisans and Alpe d'Huez."],"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T15:12:23+02:00","pinned":false,"id":1353471,"createdAt":"2026-07-24T15:12:23+02:00","stageType":"HMG","picto":"liv_polka_dot_jersey","locale":"en","slug":"claiming-the-polka-dot-jersey-at-the-eleventh-hour","publicationAt":"2026-07-24T15:12:23+02:00","stage":19,"type":"liv","highlight":true,"year":2026,"_id":"73d840fe3d7e20ea7e5a3a8c06d5f23f5630a0c0aeec437f3c1d94f12a13dc4c","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784898743906,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:claiming-the-polka-dot-jersey-at-the-eleventh-hour","model":"news"},{"stageType":"HMG","createdAt":"2026-07-24T14:15:34+02:00","slug":"stage-19-of-the-tour-de-france-2026-is-on","locale":"en","picto":"liv_actual_start","text":["The 162-man peloton just passed km 0.

It’s hard to say at this stage of the race. With the climbs of the Col Bayard and Col du Noyer right at the start, the day’s opening sets the scene for a breakaway to form, one that plenty of climbers could slip into. Given the stage's short distance, a win for the breakaway is entirely possible, indeed in recent years, it hasn't been uncommon to see the breakaway group fight for the victory in this ski resort. As for Tadej Pogacar, a win at Alpe d’Huez, via the famous 21-hairpin route, would be a prestigious achievement. However, it will be difficult for the Yellow Jersey to fully control a breakaway with his team, especially since riders like Tim Wellens and Florian Vermeersch have been struggling in recent days, on top of Brandon McNulty’s withdrawal yesterday. Even so, the battle between the breakaway and the peloton promises to be a hard-fought contest.","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","publicationAt":"2026-07-24T12:57:00+00:00","updatedAt":null},"context":{"stage":19,"category":null,"event":"tdf","locale":"en","name":"tdf","millesime":2026},"updatedBy":null,"_id":"6a636148096f7824b00d555b","createdAt":"2026-07-24T12:57:44+00:00","updatedAt":null,"createdBy":"Mouchon08","event":{"host":"https://www.letour.fr/","bubble":"cycling","trigram":"tdf","name":"Le Tour de France"}},"_id":"question-6a636148096f7824b00d555b","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"interactiveContent-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784897880597,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"interactiveContent:enundefined-undefined","model":"interactive","isClub":false},{"updatedBy":"bvittek","title":"A myth to begin with","createdBy":"bvittek","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T14:11:24+02:00","text":["Alpe d’Huez, which will be at the heart of stages 19 and 20, is a legendary climb that is hard to take your eyes off. But there are many more challenges to be faced in these two decisive days, including some ascents that made Tour history much earlier than the 21-hairpin climb.
In 1905, Col Bayard was the first Alpine pass on the route, during stage 4 (Grenoble > Toulon, 348 km). Julien Maitron was first at the summit.
Col Bayard featured in every edition between 1905 and 1921, and has been regularly used since. Today marks the 29th ascent in the history of the Tour.
On the most recent occasion, stage 17 of the Tour 2024, Magnus Cort was first at the summit. Richard Carapaz then led over Col du Noyer and took victory at Superdévoluy."],"pinned":false,"id":1353207,"createdAt":"2026-07-24T14:11:24+02:00","stageType":"HMG","picto":"liv_story","slug":"a-myth-to-begin-with","locale":"en","publicationAt":"2026-07-24T14:11:24+02:00","stage":19,"type":"liv","highlight":false,"year":2026,"_id":"9458d94ac5d16c44eb18922d1a83188a39291bdf7d111403e5c63ed6308bddcc","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784895084432,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:a-myth-to-begin-with","model":"news"},{"onlyClubTab":false,"url":"https://x.com/LeTour/status/2080636271261524387","socialContentLangs":[{"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T14:51:49+02:00","updatedBy":"ganz","createdBy":"ganz","id":21085,"locale":"fr","createdAt":"2026-07-24T14:51:05+02:00","title":"Les July: Page and Kübler","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:45:14+02:00","pinned":false,"text":["Born on 24 July 2001 in Chartres, Hugo Page is celebrating his 25th birthday on the route of the Tour de France 2026! This is his third consecutive appearance in the race, and he recently achieved his best result to date by finishing 12th in Voiron (stage 17). Hugo Page has one professional victory to his name, secured in a sprint finish on the Tour du Limousin-Périgord – Nouvelle-Aquitaine.
Ferdinand Kübler (1919–2016) was born on 24 July in Marthalen, Switzerland. Turning professional in 1940, he had to wait until the end of the Second World War to demonstrate the full extent of his abilities, which would lead him to become the first Swiss winner of the Tour de France in 1950. He also won eight stages and the points classification (1954), was world road champion (1951), a two-time winner of Liège–Bastogne–Liège (1951–52) and La Flèche Wallonne (also 1951–52), 'Ferdi' is one of the greatest Swiss cyclists in history."],"id":1353087,"_id":"4f30a2c3775dfae79895d20878c58bbb486264d0743dbf49563cf232e872cb84","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784893515137,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:24-july-page-and-kubler","model":"news"},{"url":"https://x.com/LeTour/status/2080625324912517410","id":8594,"createdAt":"2026-07-24T14:08:04+02:00","pinned":false,"updatedBy":"ganz","type":"twitter","publicationAt":"2026-07-24T14:08:04+02:00","socialContentLangs":[{"updatedBy":"ganz","locale":"fr","title":"Vers d’Huez, first leg! After Orcières-Merlette, the peloton head to another iconic venue: the first mountain top finish in the history of the Tour, and the one that has most often featured! It will be reached after the legendary 13.8 km ascent, with its 21 hairpins and its average gradient of 8.1%.
The peloton are set to roll from Gap at 14:00, to reach km 0 at 14:15. The winners will be crowned at around 17:30."],"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:30:49+02:00","updatedBy":"bvittek","title":"Welcome to the live coverage of stage 19 of the Tour de France 2026","createdBy":"bvittek","id":1353061,"_id":"3083073ecf289dd1f921c5a4af6f002bf3327a37d3539454a40f6f80779dbbf2","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784892650125,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:welcome-to-the-live-coverage-of-stage-19-of-the-tour-de-france-2026","model":"news"},{"createdAt":"2026-07-24T14:03:15+02:00","stageType":"HMG","picto":"liv_green_jersey","slug":"green-jersey-52","locale":"en","updatedBy":"bvittek","title":"Green jersey","createdBy":"bvittek","pinned":false,"text":["Battling at the front and making the most of the intermediate sprint, Mads Pedersen (Lidl-Trek) pushed his lead back up to 32 points to Jasper Philipsen (Alpecin-Premier Tech): 477 points for the Dane, 445 for the Belgian sprinter. Biniam Girmay (NSN) is still third with 361 points.
Today’s stage offers up to 45 points: 25 for the first rider at the intermediate sprint (Le Périer, km 89.4) and 20 for the stage winner."],"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T14:03:15+02:00","id":1353141,"type":"liv","highlight":false,"year":2026,"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T14:03:15+02:00","stage":19,"_id":"0b019d884039bd6a214de1dec88f697d45aa0fe032d8c0d19e9bb20c389ab399","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784894596096,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:green-jersey-52","model":"news"},{"picto":"liv_story","slug":"col-du-noyer-from-ocana-to-carapaz","locale":"en","createdAt":"2026-07-24T14:46:56+02:00","stageType":"HMG","id":1353386,"createdBy":"bvittek","title":"Col du Noyer: from Ocaña to Carapaz","updatedBy":"bvittek","text":["Tackled for the first time in 1970, Col du Noyer was the protagonist of a legendary feat the following year. At the bottom of the climb, 60 kilometres form the finish at Orcières-Merlette, Luis Ocaña distanced Lucien Van Impe, Joaquim Agostinho and Joop Zoetemelk, while Eddy Merckx was already trailing by a couple of minutes. The Spaniard flew to victory and opened massive gaps: 5’52’’ to Van Impe, second on the day, and 8’42’’ to Merckx, who fought back to the third place. Ocaña looked set to dethrone Merckx, the winner of the 1969 and 1970 editions, but he abandoned four days later after a crash on the downhill from Col de Menté.
Most recently, Col du Noyer featured in stage 17 of the Tour 2024. Richard Carapaz distanced his rivals on the climb and soloed to victory in Superdévoluy. The Ecuadorian climber is at the front again today, after his stage win at Orcières-Merlette yesterday."],"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T14:46:56+02:00","pinned":false,"year":2026,"type":"liv","highlight":false,"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T14:46:56+02:00","stage":19,"_id":"55ec141df8589b153ebb6a5eab8c05ed788955b861c5e2edcb263a004ac7c5bb","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784897216677,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:col-du-noyer-from-ocana-to-carapaz","model":"news"},{"videoLangs":[{"locale":"fr","title":"Découvrez le parcours de l'Étape 19"},{"title":"Discover the route of Stage 19","locale":"en"},{"locale":"de","title":"Discover the route of Etappe 19"},{"title":"Descubra el recorrido de la Etapa 19","locale":"es"}],"id":11514,"createdAt":"2026-07-24T08:15:00+02:00","isData":false,"isClub":false,"onlyClubTab":false,"url":"https://geo.dailymotion.com/player/xbbut.html?video=x9sikn0","onlyDataTab":false,"name":"Découvrez le parcours de l'Étape 19","_id":"83cbe3b2a4e6aca43f6c157600b46b91f7349b6caada92669daf6a19e5e11d4c","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"video-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784875478877,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"video:Découvrez le parcours de l'Étape 19","model":"video","title":"Discover the route of Stage 19","locale":"en"},{"type":"liv","highlight":false,"year":2026,"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T14:42:01+02:00","stage":19,"createdAt":"2026-07-24T14:42:01+02:00","stageType":"HMG","picto":"liv_group","locale":"en","slug":"the-leaders","title":"The 42 leaders","createdBy":"bvittek","updatedBy":"bvittek","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T14:45:41+02:00","text":["UAE Emirates RXG: Yates

The riders first rolled through Gap in the early morning of 16 July 1905, on their way from Grenoble to Toulon, where Hippolyte Aucouturier took the fourth of his five Tour stage wins.
The first time the race stopped in Gap was in 1931. Jeff Demuysere won stage 15, which started in Nice. Then Charles Pélissier conquered Grenoble, after 102 kilometres of racing from Gap.Today marks the 26th stage start in Gap, the first since 2024, when Victor Campenaerts escaped to victory in Barcelonnette. The town often gave way to stages in the Alps, including three Gap - Alpe d’Huez stages in 1991, 2006, and 2013."],"createdBy":"bvittek","title":"Gap, a historic venue of the Tour","updatedBy":"bvittek","_id":"5c29dd16f28123adcc6e606aff3d870b2e141327dd2fd4dfe4722d846431ee86","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784893047421,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:gap-a-historic-venue-of-the-tour","model":"news"},{"socialContentLangs":[{"createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","createdBy":"ganz","updatedBy":"ganz","locale":"fr","title":"Maillot blanc"},{"createdBy":"ganz","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","title":"White jersey","locale":"en","updatedBy":"ganz"},{"locale":"de","updatedBy":"ganz","title":"Weißes Trikot","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","createdBy":"ganz"}],"stage":{"timezone":"Europe/Paris","showGroups":true,"length":127.9,"podiumDisplayMode":"instantpodium","lengthDisplay":128,"stage":19,"date":"2026-07-24T00:00:00+02:00","type":"HMG","isCancelled":false},"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","highlight":false,"createdBy":"ganz","onlyDataTab":false,"onlyClubTab":false,"url":"https://www.instagram.com/p/DbK2dG-ObUh/?utm_source=ig_web_copy_link&igsh=MzRlODBiNWFlZA==","createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","id":8590,"pinned":false,"updatedBy":"ganz","embedCode":"

Paul ! While Alpe d’Huez is hosting the finishes for stages 19 and 20 this year, this is not an unprecedented occurrence. To find two consecutive finishes at the same place, we only need to look back to the start of this Tour de France, when stages 1 and 2 concluded near the Barcelona’s Olympic Stadium. Alpe d’Huez itself had already hosted two back-to-back finishes via the famous 21-hairpins route in 1979, Joaquim Agostinho winning on the seventeenth day before Joop Zoetemelk the very next day.","publicationAt":"2026-07-24T13:13:00+00:00","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","updatedAt":null},"text":"Is it unprecedented to see two consecutive stages finish in the same place ?","status":"pending"},"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T13:13:00+00:00","locale":"en","widget":"involve","_id":"question-6a6364f4d780d4eaaa077168","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"interactiveContent-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784898829163,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"interactiveContent:enundefined-undefined","model":"interactive","isClub":false},{"highlight":false,"type":"liv","year":2026,"stage":19,"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T13:54:09+02:00","stageType":"HMG","createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:54:09+02:00","locale":"en","slug":"beautiful-weather","picto":"liv_sun","pinned":false,"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:54:09+02:00","text":["The sun is shining down on the 162 riders expected in Gap, where the temperature is 30°C. Sepp Kuss (Andorra la Vella 2015)"],"id":1353540,"_id":"988e131e8e7b3dae70f99bdcce7392df5204f04ac15f7abd4fb06b74fc63adbd","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784900828469,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:11-stage-winners-at-the-front","model":"news"},{"title":"Claiming the polka-dot jersey at the eleventh hour?","createdBy":"bvittek","updatedBy":"bvittek","text":["Tadej Pogacar (UAE Emirates XRG) has been leading the mountains classification for 13 stages, since his stage win at Gavarnie-Gèdre past the Col du Tourmalet. Will the Slovenian star claim another polka-dot jersey on the Champs-Élysées, as he did in 2020, 2021, and 2025?
Valentin Paret-Peintre (Soudal Quick-Step) never gave up on the target he had set for himself before the Tour de France and, after the early ascents of Col Bayard and Col du Noyer, he is up to 84 points, while Pogacar has 70. And Richard Carapaz (EF Education-EasyPost) is a close rival: 74 points.
Since the polka-dot jersey was introduced in 1975, the winner of the KOM standings took it in the last three days of racing on six occasions. Giancarlo Bellini was the first to do so, in 1976, edging Lucien Van Impe on day 21 (penultimate stage). Then it happened five times between 2011 and 2024.
In 2020, Tadej Pogacar took the polka-dot jersey from Richard Carapaz on stage 20, while the Ecuadorian took his revenge on day 19 of the Tour 2024.
This year, everything should be decided on stage 20, with a whooping 70 KOM points on offer between Le Bourg d'Oisans and Alpe d'Huez."],"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T15:12:23+02:00","pinned":false,"id":1353471,"createdAt":"2026-07-24T15:12:23+02:00","stageType":"HMG","picto":"liv_polka_dot_jersey","locale":"en","slug":"claiming-the-polka-dot-jersey-at-the-eleventh-hour","publicationAt":"2026-07-24T15:12:23+02:00","stage":19,"type":"liv","highlight":true,"year":2026,"_id":"73d840fe3d7e20ea7e5a3a8c06d5f23f5630a0c0aeec437f3c1d94f12a13dc4c","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784898743906,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:claiming-the-polka-dot-jersey-at-the-eleventh-hour","model":"news"},{"stageType":"HMG","createdAt":"2026-07-24T14:15:34+02:00","slug":"stage-19-of-the-tour-de-france-2026-is-on","locale":"en","picto":"liv_actual_start","text":["The 162-man peloton just passed km 0.

The gap to the bunch increases to 2'55''."],"pinned":false,"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T15:01:25+02:00","title":"1 kilometre to the summit","createdBy":"bvittek","updatedBy":"bvittek","year":2026,"highlight":false,"type":"liv","stage":19,"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T15:01:25+02:00","_id":"b7a053b8f78ed6b1981e56ec0353f28447d4926075309d762a5e8d5ba76c9394","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784898085972,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:1-kilometre-to-the-summit-1","model":"news"},{"locale":"en","publicationAt":"2026-07-24T12:57:00+00:00","widget":"involve","content":{"status":"pending","text":"Can the breakaway go all the way or does Tadej Pogacar want the win ?","response":{"updatedBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","createdAt":"2026-07-24T12:58:00+00:00","text":"Hello ! It’s hard to say at this stage of the race. With the climbs of the Col Bayard and Col du Noyer right at the start, the day’s opening sets the scene for a breakaway to form, one that plenty of climbers could slip into. Given the stage's short distance, a win for the breakaway is entirely possible, indeed in recent years, it hasn't been uncommon to see the breakaway group fight for the victory in this ski resort. As for Tadej Pogacar, a win at Alpe d’Huez, via the famous 21-hairpin route, would be a prestigious achievement. However, it will be difficult for the Yellow Jersey to fully control a breakaway with his team, especially since riders like Tim Wellens and Florian Vermeersch have been struggling in recent days, on top of Brandon McNulty’s withdrawal yesterday. Even so, the battle between the breakaway and the peloton promises to be a hard-fought contest.","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","publicationAt":"2026-07-24T12:57:00+00:00","updatedAt":null},"context":{"stage":19,"category":null,"event":"tdf","locale":"en","name":"tdf","millesime":2026},"updatedBy":null,"_id":"6a636148096f7824b00d555b","createdAt":"2026-07-24T12:57:44+00:00","updatedAt":null,"createdBy":"Mouchon08","event":{"host":"https://www.letour.fr/","bubble":"cycling","trigram":"tdf","name":"Le Tour de France"}},"_id":"question-6a636148096f7824b00d555b","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"interactiveContent-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784897880597,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"interactiveContent:enundefined-undefined","model":"interactive","isClub":false},{"updatedBy":"bvittek","title":"A myth to begin with","createdBy":"bvittek","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T14:11:24+02:00","text":["Alpe d’Huez, which will be at the heart of stages 19 and 20, is a legendary climb that is hard to take your eyes off. But there are many more challenges to be faced in these two decisive days, including some ascents that made Tour history much earlier than the 21-hairpin climb.
In 1905, Col Bayard was the first Alpine pass on the route, during stage 4 (Grenoble > Toulon, 348 km). Julien Maitron was first at the summit.
Col Bayard featured in every edition between 1905 and 1921, and has been regularly used since. Today marks the 29th ascent in the history of the Tour.
On the most recent occasion, stage 17 of the Tour 2024, Magnus Cort was first at the summit. Richard Carapaz then led over Col du Noyer and took victory at Superdévoluy."],"pinned":false,"id":1353207,"createdAt":"2026-07-24T14:11:24+02:00","stageType":"HMG","picto":"liv_story","slug":"a-myth-to-begin-with","locale":"en","publicationAt":"2026-07-24T14:11:24+02:00","stage":19,"type":"liv","highlight":false,"year":2026,"_id":"9458d94ac5d16c44eb18922d1a83188a39291bdf7d111403e5c63ed6308bddcc","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784895084432,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:a-myth-to-begin-with","model":"news"},{"onlyClubTab":false,"url":"https://x.com/LeTour/status/2080636271261524387","socialContentLangs":[{"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T14:51:49+02:00","updatedBy":"ganz","createdBy":"ganz","id":21085,"locale":"fr","createdAt":"2026-07-24T14:51:05+02:00","title":"Les July: Page and Kübler","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:45:14+02:00","pinned":false,"text":["Born on 24 July 2001 in Chartres, Hugo Page is celebrating his 25th birthday on the route of the Tour de France 2026! This is his third consecutive appearance in the race, and he recently achieved his best result to date by finishing 12th in Voiron (stage 17). Hugo Page has one professional victory to his name, secured in a sprint finish on the Tour du Limousin-Périgord – Nouvelle-Aquitaine.
Ferdinand Kübler (1919–2016) was born on 24 July in Marthalen, Switzerland. Turning professional in 1940, he had to wait until the end of the Second World War to demonstrate the full extent of his abilities, which would lead him to become the first Swiss winner of the Tour de France in 1950. He also won eight stages and the points classification (1954), was world road champion (1951), a two-time winner of Liège–Bastogne–Liège (1951–52) and La Flèche Wallonne (also 1951–52), 'Ferdi' is one of the greatest Swiss cyclists in history."],"id":1353087,"_id":"4f30a2c3775dfae79895d20878c58bbb486264d0743dbf49563cf232e872cb84","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784893515137,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:24-july-page-and-kubler","model":"news"},{"url":"https://x.com/LeTour/status/2080625324912517410","id":8594,"createdAt":"2026-07-24T14:08:04+02:00","pinned":false,"updatedBy":"ganz","type":"twitter","publicationAt":"2026-07-24T14:08:04+02:00","socialContentLangs":[{"updatedBy":"ganz","locale":"fr","title":"Vers
