Will the Slovenian star claim another polka-dot jersey on the Champs-Élysées, as he did in 2020, 2021, and 2025?
Valentin Paret-Peintre (Soudal Quick-Step) never gave up on the target he had set for himself before the Tour de France and, after the early ascents of Col Bayard and Col du Noyer, he is up to 84 points, while Pogacar has 70. And Richard Carapaz (EF Education-EasyPost) is a close rival: 74 points.
Since the polka-dot jersey was introduced in 1975, the winner of the KOM standings took it in the last three days of racing on six occasions. Giancarlo Bellini was the first to do so, in 1976, edging Lucien Van Impe on day 21 (penultimate stage). Then it happened five times between 2011 and 2024.
In 2020, Tadej Pogacar took the polka-dot jersey from Richard Carapaz on stage 20, while the Ecuadorian took his revenge on day 19 of the Tour 2024.
This year, everything should be decided on stage 20, with a whooping 70 KOM points on offer between Le Bourg d'Oisans and Alpe d'Huez."],"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T15:12:23+02:00","pinned":false,"id":1353471,"createdAt":"2026-07-24T15:12:23+02:00","stageType":"HMG","picto":"liv_polka_dot_jersey","locale":"en","slug":"claiming-the-polka-dot-jersey-at-the-eleventh-hour","publicationAt":"2026-07-24T15:12:23+02:00","stage":19,"type":"liv","highlight":true,"year":2026,"_id":"73d840fe3d7e20ea7e5a3a8c06d5f23f5630a0c0aeec437f3c1d94f12a13dc4c","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784898743906,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:claiming-the-polka-dot-jersey-at-the-eleventh-hour","model":"news"},{"stageType":"HMG","createdAt":"2026-07-24T14:15:34+02:00","slug":"stage-19-of-the-tour-de-france-2026-is-on","locale":"en","picto":"liv_actual_start","text":["The 162-man peloton just passed km 0.

This is his third consecutive appearance in the race, and he recently achieved his best result to date by finishing 12th in Voiron (stage 17). Hugo Page has one professional victory to his name, secured in a sprint finish on the Tour du Limousin-Périgord – Nouvelle-Aquitaine.
Ferdinand Kübler (1919–2016) was born on 24 July in Marthalen, Switzerland. Turning professional in 1940, he had to wait until the end of the Second World War to demonstrate the full extent of his abilities, which would lead him to become the first Swiss winner of the Tour de France in 1950. He also won eight stages and the points classification (1954), was world road champion (1951), a two-time winner of Liège–Bastogne–Liège (1951–52) and La Flèche Wallonne (also 1951–52), 'Ferdi' is one of the greatest Swiss cyclists in Each day, time limits for elimination are calculated based on a coefficient reflecting the stage's difficulty and the winner's average speed. Today’s stage from Gap to L’Alpe d’Huez—deemed "highly difficult" by the organizers—carries a coefficient of 5. This results in a time limit ranging from 11% to 19% of the winner's finishing time. Given the average speed since the start, the time limit is expected to be set at 19%, or approximately 35 minutes. The group containing Tim Wellens, currently trailing the race leaders by 7 minutes, should finish within the time limit considering the distance remaining to the finish the win ?","response":{"updatedBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","createdAt":"2026-07-24T12:58:00+00:00","text":"Hello ! It’s hard to say at this stage of the race. With the climbs of the Col Bayard and Col du Noyer right at the start, the day’s opening sets the scene for a breakaway to form, one that plenty of climbers could slip into. Given the stage's short distance, a win for the breakaway is entirely possible, indeed in recent years, it hasn't been uncommon to see the breakaway group fight for the victory in this ski resort. As for Tadej Pogacar, a win at Alpe d’Huez, via the famous 21-hairpin route, would be a prestigious achievement. However, it will be difficult for the Yellow Jersey to fully control a breakaway with his team, especially since riders like Tim Wellens and Florian Vermeersch have been struggling in recent days, on top of Brandon McNulty’s withdrawal yesterday. Even so, the battle between the breakaway and the peloton promises to be a hard-fought contest.","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","publicationAt":"2026-07-24T12:57:00+00:00","updatedAt":null},"context":{"stage":19,"category":null,"event":"tdf","locale":"en","name":"tdf","millesime":2026},"updatedBy":null,"_id":"6a636148096f7824b00d555b","createdAt":"2026-07-24T12:57:44+00:00","updatedAt":null,"createdBy":"Mouchon08","event":{"host":"https://www.letour.fr/","bubble":"cycling","trigram":"tdf","name":"Le Tour de France"}},"_id":"question-6a636148096f7824b00d555b","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"interactiveContent-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784897880597,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"interactiveContent:enundefined-undefined","model":"interactive","isClub":false},{"updatedBy":"bvittek","title":"A myth to begin with","createdBy":"bvittek","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T14:11:24+02:00","text":["Alpe d’Huez, which will be at the heart of stages 19 and 20, is a legendary climb that is hard to take your eyes off. But there are many more challenges to be faced in these two decisive days, including some ascents that made Tour history much earlier than the 21-hairpin climb.
In 1905, Col Bayard was the first Alpine pass on the route, during stage 4 (Grenoble > Toulon, 348 km). Julien Maitron was first at the summit.
Col Bayard featured in every edition between 1905 and 1921, and has been regularly used since. Today marks the 29th ascent in the history of the Tour.
On the most recent occasion, stage 17 of the Tour 2024, Magnus Cort was first at the summit. Richard Carapaz then led over Col du Noyer and took victory at Superdévoluy."],"pinned":false,"id":1353207,"createdAt":"2026-07-24T14:11:24+02:00","stageType":"HMG","picto":"liv_story","slug":"a-myth-to-begin-with","locale":"en","publicationAt":"2026-07-24T14:11:24+02:00","stage":19,"type":"liv","highlight":false,"year":2026,"_id":"9458d94ac5d16c44eb18922d1a83188a39291bdf7d111403e5c63ed6308bddcc","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784895084432,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:a-myth-to-begin-with","model":"news"},{"highlight":true,"type":"liv","year":2026,"stage":19,"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T13:30:49+02:00","stageType":"HMG","createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:30:49+02:00","slug":"welcome-to-the-live-coverage-of-stage-19-of-the-tour-de-france-2026","locale":"en","picto":"liv_start","pinned":false,"text":["L’Alpe d’Huez, first leg! du Noyer: from Ocaña to Carapaz","updatedBy":"bvittek","text":["Tackled for the first time in 1970, Col du Noyer was the protagonist of a legendary feat the following year. At the bottom of the climb, 60 kilometres form the finish at Orcières-Merlette, Luis Ocaña distanced Lucien Van Impe, Joaquim Agostinho and Joop Zoetemelk, while Eddy Merckx was already trailing by a couple of minutes. The Spaniard flew to victory and opened massive gaps: 5’52’’ to Van Impe, second on the day, and 8’42’’ to Merckx, who fought back to the third place. Ocaña looked set to dethrone Merckx, the winner of the 1969 and 1970 editions, but he abandoned four days later after a crash on the downhill from Col de Menté.
Most recently, Col du Noyer featured in stage 17 of the Tour 2024. Richard Carapaz distanced his rivals on the climb and soloed to victory in Superdévoluy. The Ecuadorian climber is at the front again today, after his stage win at Orcières-Merlette yesterday."],"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T14:46:56+02:00","pinned":false,"year":2026,"type":"liv","highlight":false,"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T14:46:56+02:00","stage":19,"_id":"55ec141df8589b153ebb6a5eab8c05ed788955b861c5e2edcb263a004ac7c5bb","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784897216677,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:col-du-noyer-from-ocana-to-carapaz","model":"news"},{"socialContentLangs":[{"createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","createdBy":"ganz","updatedBy":"ganz","locale":"fr","title":"Maillot blanc"},{"createdBy":"ganz","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","title":"White jersey","locale":"en","updatedBy":"ganz"},{"locale":"de","updatedBy":"ganz","title":"Weißes Trikot","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","createdBy":"ganz"}],"stage":{"timezone":"Europe/Paris","showGroups":true,"length":127.9,"podiumDisplayMode":"instantpodium","lengthDisplay":128,"stage":19,"date":"2026-07-24T00:00:00+02:00","type":"HMG","isCancelled":false},"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","highlight":false,"createdBy":"ganz","onlyDataTab":false,"onlyClubTab":false,"url":"https://www.instagram.com/p/DbK2dG-ObUh/?utm_source=ig_web_copy_link&igsh=MzRlODBiNWFlZA==","createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","id":8590,"pinned":false,"updatedBy":"ganz","embedCode":"

The riders first rolled through Gap in the early morning of 16 July 1905, on their way from Grenoble to Toulon, where Hippolyte Aucouturier took the fourth of his five Tour stage wins.
The first time the race stopped in Gap was in 1931. Jeff Demuysere won stage 15, which started in Nice. Then Charles Pélissier conquered Grenoble, after 102 kilometres of racing from Gap.Today marks the 26th stage start in Gap, the first since 2024, when Victor Campenaerts escaped to victory in Barcelonnette. The town often gave way to stages in the Alps, including three Gap - Alpe d’Huez stages in 1991, 2006, and 2013."],"createdBy":"bvittek","title":"Gap, a historic venue of the Tour","updatedBy":"bvittek","_id":"5c29dd16f28123adcc6e606aff3d870b2e141327dd2fd4dfe4722d846431ee86","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784893047421,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:gap-a-historic-venue-of-the-tour","model":"news"},{"type":"liv","highlight":false,"year":2026,"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T14:42:01+02:00","stage":19,"createdAt":"2026-07-24T14:42:01+02:00","stageType":"HMG","picto":"liv_group","locale":"en","slug":"the-leaders","title":"The 42 leaders","createdBy":"bvittek","updatedBy":"bvittek","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T14:45:41+02:00","text":["UAE Emirates RXG: Yates

Paul ! While Alpe d’Huez is hosting the finishes for stages 19 and 20 this year, this is not an unprecedented occurrence. To find two consecutive finishes at the same place, we only need to look back to the start of this Tour de France, when stages 1 and 2 concluded near the Barcelona’s Olympic Stadium. Alpe d’Huez itself had already hosted two back-to-back finishes via the famous 21-hairpins route in 1979, Joaquim Agostinho winning on the seventeenth day before Joop Zoetemelk the very next day.","publicationAt":"2026-07-24T13:13:00+00:00","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","updatedAt":null},"text":"Is it unprecedented to see two consecutive stages finish in the same place ?","status":"pending"},"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T13:13:00+00:00","locale":"en","widget":"involve","_id":"question-6a6364f4d780d4eaaa077168","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"interactiveContent-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784898829163,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"interactiveContent:enundefined-undefined","model":"interactive","isClub":false},{"highlight":false,"type":"liv","year":2026,"stage":19,"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T13:54:09+02:00","stageType":"HMG","createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:54:09+02:00","locale":"en","slug":"beautiful-weather","picto":"liv_sun","pinned":false,"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:54:09+02:00","text":["The sun is shining down on the 162 riders expected in Gap, where the temperature is 30°C. Will the Slovenian star claim another polka-dot jersey on the Champs-Élysées, as he did in 2020, 2021, and 2025?
Valentin Paret-Peintre (Soudal Quick-Step) never gave up on the target he had set for himself before the Tour de France and, after the early ascents of Col Bayard and Col du Noyer, he is up to 84 points, while Pogacar has 70. And Richard Carapaz (EF Education-EasyPost) is a close rival: 74 points.
Since the polka-dot jersey was introduced in 1975, the winner of the KOM standings took it in the last three days of racing on six occasions. Giancarlo Bellini was the first to do so, in 1976, edging Lucien Van Impe on day 21 (penultimate stage). Then it happened five times between 2011 and 2024.
In 2020, Tadej Pogacar took the polka-dot jersey from Richard Carapaz on stage 20, while the Ecuadorian took his revenge on day 19 of the Tour 2024.
This year, everything should be decided on stage 20, with a whooping 70 KOM points on offer between Le Bourg d'Oisans and Alpe d'Huez."],"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T15:12:23+02:00","pinned":false,"id":1353471,"createdAt":"2026-07-24T15:12:23+02:00","stageType":"HMG","picto":"liv_polka_dot_jersey","locale":"en","slug":"claiming-the-polka-dot-jersey-at-the-eleventh-hour","publicationAt":"2026-07-24T15:12:23+02:00","stage":19,"type":"liv","highlight":true,"year":2026,"_id":"73d840fe3d7e20ea7e5a3a8c06d5f23f5630a0c0aeec437f3c1d94f12a13dc4c","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784898743906,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:claiming-the-polka-dot-jersey-at-the-eleventh-hour","model":"news"},{"stageType":"HMG","createdAt":"2026-07-24T14:15:34+02:00","slug":"stage-19-of-the-tour-de-france-2026-is-on","locale":"en","picto":"liv_actual_start","text":["The 162-man peloton just passed km 0.

This is his third consecutive appearance in the race, and he recently achieved his best result to date by finishing 12th in Voiron (stage 17). Hugo Page has one professional victory to his name, secured in a sprint finish on the Tour du Limousin-Périgord – Nouvelle-Aquitaine.
Ferdinand Kübler (1919–2016) was born on 24 July in Marthalen, Switzerland. Turning professional in 1940, he had to wait until the end of the Second World War to demonstrate the full extent of his abilities, which would lead him to become the first Swiss winner of the Tour de France in 1950. He also won eight stages and the points classification (1954), was world road champion (1951), a two-time winner of Liège–Bastogne–Liège (1951–52) and La Flèche Wallonne (also 1951–52), 'Ferdi' is one of the greatest Swiss cyclists in Each day, time limits for elimination are calculated based on a coefficient reflecting the stage's difficulty and the winner's average speed. Today’s stage from Gap to L’Alpe d’Huez—deemed "highly difficult" by the organizers—carries a coefficient of 5. This results in a time limit ranging from 11% to 19% of the winner's finishing time. Given the average speed since the start, the time limit is expected to be set at 19%, or approximately 35 minutes. The group containing Tim Wellens, currently trailing the race leaders by 7 minutes, should finish within the time limit considering the distance remaining to the finish Affini has withdrawn"},{"locale":"en","publicationAt":"2026-07-24T12:57:00+00:00","widget":"involve","content":{"status":"pending","text":"Can the breakaway go all the way or does Tadej Pogacar want the win ?","response":{"updatedBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","createdAt":"2026-07-24T12:58:00+00:00","text":"Hello ! It’s hard to say at this stage of the race. With the climbs of the Col Bayard and Col du Noyer right at the start, the day’s opening sets the scene for a breakaway to form, one that plenty of climbers could slip into. Given the stage's short distance, a win for the breakaway is entirely possible, indeed in recent years, it hasn't been uncommon to see the breakaway group fight for the victory in this ski resort. As for Tadej Pogacar, a win at Alpe d’Huez, via the famous 21-hairpin route, would be a prestigious achievement. However, it will be difficult for the Yellow Jersey to fully control a breakaway with his team, especially since riders like Tim Wellens and Florian Vermeersch have been struggling in recent days, on top of Brandon McNulty’s withdrawal yesterday. Even so, the battle between the breakaway and the peloton promises to be a hard-fought contest.","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","publicationAt":"2026-07-24T12:57:00+00:00","updatedAt":null},"context":{"stage":19,"category":null,"event":"tdf","locale":"en","name":"tdf","millesime":2026},"updatedBy":null,"_id":"6a636148096f7824b00d555b","createdAt":"2026-07-24T12:57:44+00:00","updatedAt":null,"createdBy":"Mouchon08","event":{"host":"https://www.letour.fr/","bubble":"cycling","trigram":"tdf","name":"Le Tour de France"}},"_id":"question-6a636148096f7824b00d555b","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"interactiveContent-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784897880597,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"interactiveContent:enundefined-undefined","model":"interactive","isClub":false},{"updatedBy":"bvittek","title":"A myth to begin with","createdBy":"bvittek","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T14:11:24+02:00","text":["Alpe d’Huez, which will be at the heart of stages 19 and 20, is a legendary climb that is hard to take your eyes off. But there are many more challenges to be faced in these two decisive days, including some ascents that made Tour history much earlier than the 21-hairpin climb.
In 1905, Col Bayard was the first Alpine pass on the route, during stage 4 (Grenoble > Toulon, 348 km). Julien Maitron was first at the summit.
Col Bayard featured in every edition between 1905 and 1921, and has been regularly used since. Today marks the 29th ascent in the history of the Tour.
On the most recent occasion, stage 17 of the Tour 2024, Magnus Cort was first at the summit. Richard Carapaz then led over Col du Noyer and took victory at Superdévoluy."],"pinned":false,"id":1353207,"createdAt":"2026-07-24T14:11:24+02:00","stageType":"HMG","picto":"liv_story","slug":"a-myth-to-begin-with","locale":"en","publicationAt":"2026-07-24T14:11:24+02:00","stage":19,"type":"liv","highlight":false,"year":2026,"_id":"9458d94ac5d16c44eb18922d1a83188a39291bdf7d111403e5c63ed6308bddcc","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784895084432,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:a-myth-to-begin-with","model":"news"},{"slug":"pinarello-q36-5-participate-in-the-chase","locale":"en","stageType":"HMG","createdAt":"2026-07-24T16:11:34+02:00","id":1353599,"pictoTeam":"https://img.aso.fr/core_app/img-cycling-tdf-png/pqt/72277/0:0,400:400-80-0-100/dd122","pinned":false,"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T16:11:34+02:00","text":["Meanwhile, Ben Healy (EF Education-EasyPost) is dropped from the breakaway, who are 6 km fromthe intermediate sprint."],"title":"Pinarello Q36.5 participate in the chase","createdBy":"bvittek","updatedBy":"bvittek","year":2026,"highlight":false,"type":"liv","stage":19,"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T16:11:34+02:00","_id":"1b4042de3d38dbc174f881016fb28a7ec583b1d72b5442ca27b9a7453b612f45","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784902294754,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:pinarello-q36-5-participate-in-the-chase","model":"news"},{"highlight":true,"type":"liv","year":2026,"stage":19,"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T13:30:49+02:00","stageType":"HMG","createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:30:49+02:00","slug":"welcome-to-the-live-coverage-of-stage-19-of-the-tour-de-france-2026","locale":"en","picto":"liv_start","pinned":false,"text":["L’Alpe d’Huez, first leg! du Noyer: from Ocaña to Carapaz","updatedBy":"bvittek","text":["Tackled for the first time in 1970, Col du Noyer was the protagonist of a legendary feat the following year. At the bottom of the climb, 60 kilometres form the finish at Orcières-Merlette, Luis Ocaña distanced Lucien Van Impe, Joaquim Agostinho and Joop Zoetemelk, while Eddy Merckx was already trailing by a couple of minutes. The Spaniard flew to victory and opened massive gaps: 5’52’’ to Van Impe, second on the day, and 8’42’’ to Merckx, who fought back to the third place. Ocaña looked set to dethrone Merckx, the winner of the 1969 and 1970 editions, but he abandoned four days later after a crash on the downhill from Col de Menté.
Most recently, Col du Noyer featured in stage 17 of the Tour 2024. Richard Carapaz distanced his rivals on the climb and soloed to victory in Superdévoluy. The Ecuadorian climber is at the front again today, after his stage win at Orcières-Merlette yesterday."],"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T14:46:56+02:00","pinned":false,"year":2026,"type":"liv","highlight":false,"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T14:46:56+02:00","stage":19,"_id":"55ec141df8589b153ebb6a5eab8c05ed788955b861c5e2edcb263a004ac7c5bb","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784897216677,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:col-du-noyer-from-ocana-to-carapaz","model":"news"},{"socialContentLangs":[{"createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","createdBy":"ganz","updatedBy":"ganz","locale":"fr","title":"Maillot blanc"},{"createdBy":"ganz","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","title":"White jersey","locale":"en","updatedBy":"ganz"},{"locale":"de","updatedBy":"ganz","title":"Weißes Trikot","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","createdBy":"ganz"}],"stage":{"timezone":"Europe/Paris","showGroups":true,"length":127.9,"podiumDisplayMode":"instantpodium","lengthDisplay":128,"stage":19,"date":"2026-07-24T00:00:00+02:00","type":"HMG","isCancelled":false},"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","highlight":false,"createdBy":"ganz","onlyDataTab":false,"onlyClubTab":false,"url":"https://www.instagram.com/p/DbK2dG-ObUh/?utm_source=ig_web_copy_link&igsh=MzRlODBiNWFlZA==","createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","id":8590,"pinned":false,"updatedBy":"ganz","embedCode":"

The riders first rolled through Gap in the early morning of 16 July 1905, on their way from Grenoble to Toulon, where Hippolyte Aucouturier took the fourth of his five Tour stage wins.
The first time the race stopped in Gap was in 1931. Jeff Demuysere won stage 15, which started in Nice. Then Charles Pélissier conquered Grenoble, after 102 kilometres of racing from Gap.Today marks the 26th stage start in Gap, the first since 2024, when Victor Campenaerts escaped to victory in Barcelonnette. The town often gave way to stages in the Alps, including three Gap - Alpe d’Huez stages in 1991, 2006, and 2013."],"createdBy":"bvittek","title":"Gap, a historic venue of the Tour","updatedBy":"bvittek","_id":"5c29dd16f28123adcc6e606aff3d870b2e141327dd2fd4dfe4722d846431ee86","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784893047421,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:gap-a-historic-venue-of-the-tour","model":"news"},{"type":"liv","highlight":false,"year":2026,"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T14:42:01+02:00","stage":19,"createdAt":"2026-07-24T14:42:01+02:00","stageType":"HMG","picto":"liv_group","locale":"en","slug":"the-leaders","title":"The 42 leaders","createdBy":"bvittek","updatedBy":"bvittek","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T14:45:41+02:00","text":["UAE Emirates RXG: Yates

While Alpe d’Huez is hosting the finishes for stages 19 and 20 this year, this is not an unprecedented occurrence. To find two consecutive finishes at the same place, we only need to look back to the start of this Tour de France, when stages 1 and 2 concluded near the Barcelona’s Olympic Stadium. Alpe d’Huez itself had already hosted two back-to-back finishes via the famous 21-hairpins route in 1979, Joaquim Agostinho winning on the seventeenth day before Joop Zoetemelk the very next day.","publicationAt":"2026-07-24T13:13:00+00:00","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","updatedAt":null},"text":"Is it unprecedented to see two consecutive stages finish in the same place ?","status":"pending"},"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T13:13:00+00:00","locale":"en","widget":"involve","_id":"question-6a6364f4d780d4eaaa077168","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"interactiveContent-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784898829163,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"interactiveContent:enundefined-undefined","model":"interactive","isClub":false},{"highlight":false,"type":"liv","year":2026,"stage":19,"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T13:54:09+02:00","stageType":"HMG","createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:54:09+02:00","locale":"en","slug":"beautiful-weather","picto":"liv_sun","pinned":false,"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:54:09+02:00","text":["The sun is shining down on the 162 riders expected in Gap, where the temperature is 30°C. Will the Slovenian star claim another polka-dot jersey on the Champs-Élysées, as he did in 2020, 2021, and 2025?
Valentin Paret-Peintre (Soudal Quick-Step) never gave up on the target he had set for himself before the Tour de France and, after the early ascents of Col Bayard and Col du Noyer, he is up to 84 points, while Pogacar has 70. And Richard Carapaz (EF Education-EasyPost) is a close rival: 74 points.
Since the polka-dot jersey was introduced in 1975, the winner of the KOM standings took it in the last three days of racing on six occasions. Giancarlo Bellini was the first to do so, in 1976, edging Lucien Van Impe on day 21 (penultimate stage). Then it happened five times between 2011 and 2024.
In 2020, Tadej Pogacar took the polka-dot jersey from Richard Carapaz on stage 20, while the Ecuadorian took his revenge on day 19 of the Tour 2024.
This year, everything should be decided on stage 20, with a whooping 70 KOM points on offer between Le Bourg d'Oisans and Alpe d'Huez."],"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T15:12:23+02:00","pinned":false,"id":1353471,"createdAt":"2026-07-24T15:12:23+02:00","stageType":"HMG","picto":"liv_polka_dot_jersey","locale":"en","slug":"claiming-the-polka-dot-jersey-at-the-eleventh-hour","publicationAt":"2026-07-24T15:12:23+02:00","stage":19,"type":"liv","highlight":true,"year":2026,"_id":"73d840fe3d7e20ea7e5a3a8c06d5f23f5630a0c0aeec437f3c1d94f12a13dc4c","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784898743906,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:claiming-the-polka-dot-jersey-at-the-eleventh-hour","model":"news"},{"stageType":"HMG","createdAt":"2026-07-24T14:15:34+02:00","slug":"stage-19-of-the-tour-de-france-2026-is-on","locale":"en","picto":"liv_actual_start","text":["The 162-man peloton just passed km 0.

This is his third consecutive appearance in the race, and he recently achieved his best result to date by finishing 12th in Voiron (stage 17). Hugo Page has one professional victory to his name, secured in a sprint finish on the Tour du Limousin-Périgord – Nouvelle-Aquitaine.
Ferdinand Kübler (1919–2016) was born on 24 July in Marthalen, Switzerland. Turning professional in 1940, he had to wait until the end of the Second World War to demonstrate the full extent of his abilities, which would lead him to become the first Swiss winner of the Tour de France in 1950. He also won eight stages and the points classification (1954), was world road champion (1951), a two-time winner of Liège–Bastogne–Liège (1951–52) and La Flèche Wallonne (also 1951–52), 'Ferdi' is one of the greatest Swiss cyclists in Will the Wellens group be able to finish within the time limit ?","updatedBy":null,"event":{"host":"https://www.letour.fr/","name":"Le Tour de France","bubble":"cycling","trigram":"tdf"},"updatedAt":null,"createdAt":"2026-07-24T14:10:22+00:00","response":{"text":"Hello ! Each day, time limits for elimination are calculated based on a coefficient reflecting the stage's difficulty and the winner's average speed. Today’s stage from Gap to L’Alpe d’Huez—deemed "highly difficult" by the organizers—carries a coefficient of 5. This results in a time limit ranging from 11% to 19% of the winner's finishing time. Given the average speed since the start, the time limit is expected to be set at 19%, or approximately 35 minutes. The group containing Tim Wellens, currently trailing the race leaders by 7 minutes, should finish within the time limit considering the distance remaining to the finish Affini has withdrawn"},{"locale":"en","publicationAt":"2026-07-24T12:57:00+00:00","widget":"involve","content":{"status":"pending","text":"Can the breakaway go all the way or does Tadej Pogacar want the win ?","response":{"updatedBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","createdAt":"2026-07-24T12:58:00+00:00","text":"Hello ! It’s hard to say at this stage of the race. With the climbs of the Col Bayard and Col du Noyer right at the start, the day’s opening sets the scene for a breakaway to form, one that plenty of climbers could slip into. Given the stage's short distance, a win for the breakaway is entirely possible, indeed in recent years, it hasn't been uncommon to see the breakaway group fight for the victory in this ski resort. As for Tadej Pogacar, a win at Alpe d’Huez, via the famous 21-hairpin route, would be a prestigious achievement. However, it will be difficult for the Yellow Jersey to fully control a breakaway with his team, especially since riders like Tim Wellens and Florian Vermeersch have been struggling in recent days, on top of Brandon McNulty’s withdrawal yesterday. Even so, the battle between the breakaway and the peloton promises to be a hard-fought contest.","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","publicationAt":"2026-07-24T12:57:00+00:00","updatedAt":null},"context":{"stage":19,"category":null,"event":"tdf","locale":"en","name":"tdf","millesime":2026},"updatedBy":null,"_id":"6a636148096f7824b00d555b","createdAt":"2026-07-24T12:57:44+00:00","updatedAt":null,"createdBy":"Mouchon08","event":{"host":"https://www.letour.fr/","bubble":"cycling","trigram":"tdf","name":"Le Tour de France"}},"_id":"question-6a636148096f7824b00d555b","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"interactiveContent-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784897880597,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"interactiveContent:enundefined-undefined","model":"interactive","isClub":false},{"updatedBy":"bvittek","title":"A myth to begin with","createdBy":"bvittek","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T14:11:24+02:00","text":["Alpe d’Huez, which will be at the heart of stages 19 and 20, is a legendary climb that is hard to take your eyes off. But there are many more challenges to be faced in these two decisive days, including some ascents that made Tour history much earlier than the 21-hairpin climb.
In 1905, Col Bayard was the first Alpine pass on the route, during stage 4 (Grenoble > Toulon, 348 km). Julien Maitron was first at the summit.
Col Bayard featured in every edition between 1905 and 1921, and has been regularly used since. Today marks the 29th ascent in the history of the Tour.
On the most recent occasion, stage 17 of the Tour 2024, Magnus Cort was first at the summit. Richard Carapaz then led over Col du Noyer and took victory at Superdévoluy."],"pinned":false,"id":1353207,"createdAt":"2026-07-24T14:11:24+02:00","stageType":"HMG","picto":"liv_story","slug":"a-myth-to-begin-with","locale":"en","publicationAt":"2026-07-24T14:11:24+02:00","stage":19,"type":"liv","highlight":false,"year":2026,"_id":"9458d94ac5d16c44eb18922d1a83188a39291bdf7d111403e5c63ed6308bddcc","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784895084432,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:a-myth-to-begin-with","model":"news"},{"highlight":false,"type":"liv","year":2026,"stage":19,"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T16:11:34+02:00","stageType":"HMG","createdAt":"2026-07-24T16:11:34+02:00","locale":"en","slug":"pinarello-q36-5-participate-in-the-chase","pictoTeam":"https://img.aso.fr/core_app/img-cycling-tdf-png/pqt/72277/0:0,400:400-80-0-100/dd122","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T16:12:32+02:00","text":["Meanwhile, Ben Healy (EF Education-EasyPost) is dropped from the breakaway, who are 6 km from the intermediate sprint."],"pinned":false,"title":"Pinarello Q36.5 participate in the chase","createdBy":"bvittek","updatedBy":"bvittek","id":1353599,"_id":"1b4042de3d38dbc174f881016fb28a7ec583b1d72b5442ca27b9a7453b612f45","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784902352666,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:pinarello-q36-5-participate-in-the-chase","model":"news"},{"highlight":true,"type":"liv","year":2026,"stage":19,"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T13:30:49+02:00","stageType":"HMG","createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:30:49+02:00","slug":"welcome-to-the-live-coverage-of-stage-19-of-the-tour-de-france-2026","locale":"en","picto":"liv_start","pinned":false,"text":["L’Alpe d’Huez, first leg! du Noyer: from Ocaña to Carapaz","updatedBy":"bvittek","text":["Tackled for the first time in 1970, Col du Noyer was the protagonist of a legendary feat the following year. At the bottom of the climb, 60 kilometres form the finish at Orcières-Merlette, Luis Ocaña distanced Lucien Van Impe, Joaquim Agostinho and Joop Zoetemelk, while Eddy Merckx was already trailing by a couple of minutes. The Spaniard flew to victory and opened massive gaps: 5’52’’ to Van Impe, second on the day, and 8’42’’ to Merckx, who fought back to the third place. Ocaña looked set to dethrone Merckx, the winner of the 1969 and 1970 editions, but he abandoned four days later after a crash on the downhill from Col de Menté.
Most recently, Col du Noyer featured in stage 17 of the Tour 2024. Richard Carapaz distanced his rivals on the climb and soloed to victory in Superdévoluy. The Ecuadorian climber is at the front again today, after his stage win at Orcières-Merlette yesterday."],"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T14:46:56+02:00","pinned":false,"year":2026,"type":"liv","highlight":false,"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T14:46:56+02:00","stage":19,"_id":"55ec141df8589b153ebb6a5eab8c05ed788955b861c5e2edcb263a004ac7c5bb","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784897216677,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:col-du-noyer-from-ocana-to-carapaz","model":"news"},{"socialContentLangs":[{"createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","createdBy":"ganz","updatedBy":"ganz","locale":"fr","title":"Maillot blanc"},{"createdBy":"ganz","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","title":"White jersey","locale":"en","updatedBy":"ganz"},{"locale":"de","updatedBy":"ganz","title":"Weißes Trikot","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","createdBy":"ganz"}],"stage":{"timezone":"Europe/Paris","showGroups":true,"length":127.9,"podiumDisplayMode":"instantpodium","lengthDisplay":128,"stage":19,"date":"2026-07-24T00:00:00+02:00","type":"HMG","isCancelled":false},"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","highlight":false,"createdBy":"ganz","onlyDataTab":false,"onlyClubTab":false,"url":"https://www.instagram.com/p/DbK2dG-ObUh/?utm_source=ig_web_copy_link&igsh=MzRlODBiNWFlZA==","createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","id":8590,"pinned":false,"updatedBy":"ganz","embedCode":"

The riders first rolled through Gap in the early morning of 16 July 1905, on their way from Grenoble to Toulon, where Hippolyte Aucouturier took the fourth of his five Tour stage wins.
The first time the race stopped in Gap was in 1931. Jeff Demuysere won stage 15, which started in Nice. Then Charles Pélissier conquered Grenoble, after 102 kilometres of racing from Gap.Today marks the 26th stage start in Gap, the first since 2024, when Victor Campenaerts escaped to victory in Barcelonnette. The town often gave way to stages in the Alps, including three Gap - Alpe d’Huez stages in 1991, 2006, and 2013."],"createdBy":"bvittek","title":"Gap, a historic venue of the Tour","updatedBy":"bvittek","_id":"5c29dd16f28123adcc6e606aff3d870b2e141327dd2fd4dfe4722d846431ee86","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784893047421,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:gap-a-historic-venue-of-the-tour","model":"news"},{"type":"liv","highlight":false,"year":2026,"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T14:42:01+02:00","stage":19,"createdAt":"2026-07-24T14:42:01+02:00","stageType":"HMG","picto":"liv_group","locale":"en","slug":"the-leaders","title":"The 42 leaders","createdBy":"bvittek","updatedBy":"bvittek","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T14:45:41+02:00","text":["UAE Emirates RXG: Yates

While Alpe d’Huez is hosting the finishes for stages 19 and 20 this year, this is not an unprecedented occurrence. To find two consecutive finishes at the same place, we only need to look back to the start of this Tour de France, when stages 1 and 2 concluded near the Barcelona’s Olympic Stadium. Alpe d’Huez itself had already hosted two back-to-back finishes via the famous 21-hairpins route in 1979, Joaquim Agostinho winning on the seventeenth day before Joop Zoetemelk the very next day.","publicationAt":"2026-07-24T13:13:00+00:00","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","updatedAt":null},"text":"Is it unprecedented to see two consecutive stages finish in the same place ?","status":"pending"},"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T13:13:00+00:00","locale":"en","widget":"involve","_id":"question-6a6364f4d780d4eaaa077168","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"interactiveContent-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784898829163,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"interactiveContent:enundefined-undefined","model":"interactive","isClub":false},{"highlight":false,"type":"liv","year":2026,"stage":19,"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T13:54:09+02:00","stageType":"HMG","createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:54:09+02:00","locale":"en","slug":"beautiful-weather","picto":"liv_sun","pinned":false,"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:54:09+02:00","text":["The sun is shining down on the 162 riders expected in Gap, where the temperature is 30°C. Will the Slovenian star claim another polka-dot jersey on the Champs-Élysées, as he did in 2020, 2021, and 2025?
Valentin Paret-Peintre (Soudal Quick-Step) never gave up on the target he had set for himself before the Tour de France and, after the early ascents of Col Bayard and Col du Noyer, he is up to 84 points, while Pogacar has 70. And Richard Carapaz (EF Education-EasyPost) is a close rival: 74 points.
Since the polka-dot jersey was introduced in 1975, the winner of the KOM standings took it in the last three days of racing on six occasions. Giancarlo Bellini was the first to do so, in 1976, edging Lucien Van Impe on day 21 (penultimate stage). Then it happened five times between 2011 and 2024.
In 2020, Tadej Pogacar took the polka-dot jersey from Richard Carapaz on stage 20, while the Ecuadorian took his revenge on day 19 of the Tour 2024.
This year, everything should be decided on stage 20, with a whooping 70 KOM points on offer between Le Bourg d'Oisans and Alpe d'Huez."],"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T15:12:23+02:00","pinned":false,"id":1353471,"createdAt":"2026-07-24T15:12:23+02:00","stageType":"HMG","picto":"liv_polka_dot_jersey","locale":"en","slug":"claiming-the-polka-dot-jersey-at-the-eleventh-hour","publicationAt":"2026-07-24T15:12:23+02:00","stage":19,"type":"liv","highlight":true,"year":2026,"_id":"73d840fe3d7e20ea7e5a3a8c06d5f23f5630a0c0aeec437f3c1d94f12a13dc4c","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784898743906,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:claiming-the-polka-dot-jersey-at-the-eleventh-hour","model":"news"},{"stageType":"HMG","createdAt":"2026-07-24T14:15:34+02:00","slug":"stage-19-of-the-tour-de-france-2026-is-on","locale":"en","picto":"liv_actual_start","text":["The 162-man peloton just passed km 0.

This is his third consecutive appearance in the race, and he recently achieved his best result to date by finishing 12th in Voiron (stage 17). Hugo Page has one professional victory to his name, secured in a sprint finish on the Tour du Limousin-Périgord – Nouvelle-Aquitaine.
Ferdinand Kübler (1919–2016) was born on 24 July in Marthalen, Switzerland. Turning professional in 1940, he had to wait until the end of the Second World War to demonstrate the full extent of his abilities, which would lead him to become the first Swiss winner of the Tour de France in 1950. He also won eight stages and the points classification (1954), was world road champion (1951), a two-time winner of Liège–Bastogne–Liège (1951–52) and La Flèche Wallonne (also 1951–52), 'Ferdi' is one of the greatest Swiss cyclists in jersey","createdBy":"bvittek","pinned":false,"text":["Battling at the front and making the most of the intermediate sprint, Mads Pedersen (Lidl-Trek) pushed his lead back up to 32 points to Jasper Philipsen (Alpecin-Premier Tech): 477 points for the Dane, 445 for the Belgian sprinter. Will the Wellens group be able to finish within the time limit ?","updatedBy":null,"event":{"host":"https://www.letour.fr/","name":"Le Tour de France","bubble":"cycling","trigram":"tdf"},"updatedAt":null,"createdAt":"2026-07-24T14:10:22+00:00","response":{"text":"Hello ! Each day, time limits for elimination are calculated based on a coefficient reflecting the stage's difficulty and the winner's average speed. Today’s stage from Gap to L’Alpe d’Huez—deemed "highly difficult" by the organizers—carries a coefficient of 5. This results in a time limit ranging from 11% to 19% of the winner's finishing time. Given the average speed since the start, the time limit is expected to be set at 19%, or approximately 35 minutes. The group containing Tim Wellens, currently trailing the race leaders by 7 minutes, should finish within the time limit considering the distance remaining to the finish Affini has withdrawn"},{"locale":"en","publicationAt":"2026-07-24T12:57:00+00:00","widget":"involve","content":{"status":"pending","text":"Can the breakaway go all the way or does Tadej Pogacar want the win ?","response":{"updatedBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","createdAt":"2026-07-24T12:58:00+00:00","text":"Hello ! It’s hard to say at this stage of the race. With the climbs of the Col Bayard and Col du Noyer right at the start, the day’s opening sets the scene for a breakaway to form, one that plenty of climbers could slip into. Given the stage's short distance, a win for the breakaway is entirely possible, indeed in recent years, it hasn't been uncommon to see the breakaway group fight for the victory in this ski resort. As for Tadej Pogacar, a win at Alpe d’Huez, via the famous 21-hairpin route, would be a prestigious achievement. However, it will be difficult for the Yellow Jersey to fully control a breakaway with his team, especially since riders like Tim Wellens and Florian Vermeersch have been struggling in recent days, on top of Brandon McNulty’s withdrawal yesterday. Even so, the battle between the breakaway and the peloton promises to be a hard-fought contest.","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","publicationAt":"2026-07-24T12:57:00+00:00","updatedAt":null},"context":{"stage":19,"category":null,"event":"tdf","locale":"en","name":"tdf","millesime":2026},"updatedBy":null,"_id":"6a636148096f7824b00d555b","createdAt":"2026-07-24T12:57:44+00:00","updatedAt":null,"createdBy":"Mouchon08","event":{"host":"https://www.letour.fr/","bubble":"cycling","trigram":"tdf","name":"Le Tour de France"}},"_id":"question-6a636148096f7824b00d555b","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"interactiveContent-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784897880597,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"interactiveContent:enundefined-undefined","model":"interactive","isClub":false},{"updatedBy":"bvittek","title":"A myth to begin with","createdBy":"bvittek","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T14:11:24+02:00","text":["Alpe d’Huez, which will be at the heart of stages 19 and 20, is a legendary climb that is hard to take your eyes off. But there are many more challenges to be faced in these two decisive days, including some ascents that made Tour history much earlier than the 21-hairpin climb.
In 1905, Col Bayard was the first Alpine pass on the route, during stage 4 (Grenoble > Toulon, 348 km). Julien Maitron was first at the summit.
Col Bayard featured in every edition between 1905 and 1921, and has been regularly used since. Today marks the 29th ascent in the history of the Tour.
On the most recent occasion, stage 17 of the Tour 2024, Magnus Cort was first at the summit. du Noyer: from Ocaña to Carapaz","updatedBy":"bvittek","text":["Tackled for the first time in 1970, Col du Noyer was the protagonist of a legendary feat the following year. At the bottom of the climb, 60 kilometres form the finish at Orcières-Merlette, Luis Ocaña distanced Lucien Van Impe, Joaquim Agostinho and Joop Zoetemelk, while Eddy Merckx was already trailing by a couple of minutes. The Spaniard flew to victory and opened massive gaps: 5’52’’ to Van Impe, second on the day, and 8’42’’ to Merckx, who fought back to the third place. Ocaña looked set to dethrone Merckx, the winner of the 1969 and 1970 editions, but he abandoned four days later after a crash on the downhill from Col de Menté.
Most recently, Col du Noyer featured in stage 17 of the Tour 2024. Richard Carapaz distanced his rivals on the climb and soloed to victory in Superdévoluy. The Ecuadorian climber is at the front again today, after his stage win at Orcières-Merlette yesterday."],"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T14:46:56+02:00","pinned":false,"year":2026,"type":"liv","highlight":false,"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T14:46:56+02:00","stage":19,"_id":"55ec141df8589b153ebb6a5eab8c05ed788955b861c5e2edcb263a004ac7c5bb","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784897216677,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:col-du-noyer-from-ocana-to-carapaz","model":"news"},{"socialContentLangs":[{"createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","createdBy":"ganz","updatedBy":"ganz","locale":"fr","title":"Maillot blanc"},{"createdBy":"ganz","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","title":"White jersey","locale":"en","updatedBy":"ganz"},{"locale":"de","updatedBy":"ganz","title":"Weißes Trikot","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","createdBy":"ganz"}],"stage":{"timezone":"Europe/Paris","showGroups":true,"length":127.9,"podiumDisplayMode":"instantpodium","lengthDisplay":128,"stage":19,"date":"2026-07-24T00:00:00+02:00","type":"HMG","isCancelled":false},"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","highlight":false,"createdBy":"ganz","onlyDataTab":false,"onlyClubTab":false,"url":"https://www.instagram.com/p/DbK2dG-ObUh/?utm_source=ig_web_copy_link&igsh=MzRlODBiNWFlZA==","createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","id":8590,"pinned":false,"updatedBy":"ganz","embedCode":"

The riders first rolled through Gap in the early morning of 16 July 1905, on their way from Grenoble to Toulon, where Hippolyte Aucouturier took the fourth of his five Tour stage wins.
The first time the race stopped in Gap was in 1931. Jeff Demuysere won stage 15, which started in Nice. Then Charles Pélissier conquered Grenoble, after 102 kilometres of racing from Gap.Today marks the 26th stage start in Gap, the first since 2024, when Victor Campenaerts escaped to victory in Barcelonnette. The town often gave way to stages in the Alps, including three Gap - Alpe d’Huez stages in 1991, 2006, and 2013."],"createdBy":"bvittek","title":"Gap, a historic venue of the Tour","updatedBy":"bvittek","_id":"5c29dd16f28123adcc6e606aff3d870b2e141327dd2fd4dfe4722d846431ee86","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784893047421,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:gap-a-historic-venue-of-the-tour","model":"news"},{"type":"liv","highlight":false,"year":2026,"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T14:42:01+02:00","stage":19,"createdAt":"2026-07-24T14:42:01+02:00","stageType":"HMG","picto":"liv_group","locale":"en","slug":"the-leaders","title":"The 42 leaders","createdBy":"bvittek","updatedBy":"bvittek","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T14:45:41+02:00","text":["UAE Emirates RXG: Yates

the win ?","response":{"updatedBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","createdAt":"2026-07-24T12:58:00+00:00","text":"Hello ! It’s hard to say at this stage of the race. With the climbs of the Col Bayard and Col du Noyer right at the start, the day’s opening sets the scene for a breakaway to form, one that plenty of climbers could slip into. Given the stage's short distance, a win for the breakaway is entirely possible, indeed in recent years, it hasn't been uncommon to see the breakaway group fight for the victory in this ski resort. As for Tadej Pogacar, a win at Alpe d’Huez, via the famous 21-hairpin route, would be a prestigious achievement. However, it will be difficult for the Yellow Jersey to fully control a breakaway with his team, especially since riders like Tim Wellens and Florian Vermeersch have been struggling in recent days, on top of Brandon McNulty’s withdrawal yesterday. Even so, the battle between the breakaway and the peloton promises to be a hard-fought contest.","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","publicationAt":"2026-07-24T12:57:00+00:00","updatedAt":null},"context":{"stage":19,"category":null,"event":"tdf","locale":"en","name":"tdf","millesime":2026},"updatedBy":null,"_id":"6a636148096f7824b00d555b","createdAt":"2026-07-24T12:57:44+00:00","updatedAt":null,"createdBy":"Mouchon08","event":{"host":"https://www.letour.fr/","bubble":"cycling","trigram":"tdf","name":"Le Tour de France"}},"_id":"question-6a636148096f7824b00d555b","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"interactiveContent-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784897880597,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"interactiveContent:enundefined-undefined","model":"interactive","isClub":false},{"type":"liv","highlight":false,"pictoNationalTeam":"liv_english_jersey","year":2026,"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T16:49:16+02:00","stage":19,"createdAt":"2026-07-24T16:49:16+02:00","stageType":"HMG","slug":"2022-pidcock-takes-alpe-dhuez-glory","locale":"en","updatedBy":"bvittek","createdBy":"bvittek","title":"2022: Pidcock takes Alpe d’Huez glory","text":["The last finish at Alpe d’Huez before this year’s double visit was four years ago, on stage 12 of the 2022 edition. It was 14 July and Tom Pidcock delivered the fireworks.
The stage started from Briançon and headed immediately to Col du Galibier, a magnificent summit to battle for the breakaway. Climbers set off on the ascent… and Tom Pidcock attacked towards the summit. The British star joined the front of the race with a most spectacular downhill. He made a first selection on the following ascent to Col de la Croix-de-Fer. And he dropped the rest of his rivals with a decisive attack 10.6 kilometres from the summit. A couple of weeks before his 23rd birthday, he became the youngest winner at Alpe d’Huez.
Tadej Pogacar was the first of the GC contenders, level on time with Jonas Vingegaard, who was wearing the Maillot Jaune en route to his first Tour de France overall victory. Geraint Thomas, the winner here in 2018, finished with them at Alpe d’Huez and eventually stood with them on the final podium in Paris."],"pinned":false,"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T16:49:16+02:00","id":1353790,"_id":"902558f697f210001ee9bb018cda28ad36af24707847fd556ac7d3979ab7e5e6","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784904556969,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:2022-pidcock-takes-alpe-dhuez-glory","model":"news"},{"highlight":false,"updatedBy":"ganz","url":"https://x.com/LeTour/status/2080661454416396708","createdBy":"ganz","onlyDataTab":false,"pinned":false,"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T16:29:00+02:00","onlyClubTab":false,"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T16:30:39+02:00","type":"twitter","stage":{"stage":19,"podiumDisplayMode":"instantpodium","length":127.9,"date":"2026-07-24T00:00:00+02:00","lengthDisplay":128,"isCancelled":false,"type":"HMG","showGroups":true,"timezone":"Europe/Paris"},"id":8603,"createdAt":"2026-07-24T16:30:39+02:00","socialContentLangs":[{"createdAt":"2026-07-24T16:30:39+02:00","title":"Valentin Paret-Peintre has been dropped from the breakaway","locale":"en"},{"updatedBy":"bvittek","title":"A myth to begin with","createdBy":"bvittek","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T14:11:24+02:00","text":["Alpe d’Huez, which will be at the heart of stages 19 and 20, is a legendary climb that is hard to take your eyes off. But there are many more challenges to be faced in these two decisive days, including some ascents that made Tour history much earlier than the 21-hairpin climb.
In 1905, Col Bayard was the first Alpine pass on the route, during stage 4 (Grenoble > Toulon, 348 km). Julien Maitron was first at the summit.
Col Bayard featured in every edition between 1905 and 1921, and has been regularly used since. Today marks the 29th ascent in the history of the Tour.
On the most recent occasion, stage 17 of the Tour 2024, Magnus Cort was first at the summit. Richard Carapaz then led over Col du Noyer and took victory at Superdévoluy."],"pinned":false,"id":1353207,"createdAt":"2026-07-24T14:11:24+02:00","stageType":"HMG","picto":"liv_story","slug":"a-myth-to-begin-with","locale":"en","publicationAt":"2026-07-24T14:11:24+02:00","stage":19,"type":"liv","highlight":false,"year":2026,"_id":"9458d94ac5d16c44eb18922d1a83188a39291bdf7d111403e5c63ed6308bddcc","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784895084432,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:a-myth-to-begin-with","model":"news"},{"stage":19,"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T16:35:00+02:00","highlight":false,"type":"liv","year":2026,"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T16:35:00+02:00","pinned":false,"text":["Now Derek Gee-West sets the pace in the bunch, after he participted in opening the gaps for the breakaway.

away","locale":"en"},{"picto":"liv_story","slug":"col-du-noyer-from-ocana-to-carapaz","locale":"en","createdAt":"2026-07-24T14:46:56+02:00","stageType":"HMG","id":1353386,"createdBy":"bvittek","title":"Col du Noyer: from Ocaña to Carapaz","updatedBy":"bvittek","text":["Tackled for the first time in 1970, Col du Noyer was the protagonist of a legendary feat the following year. At the bottom of the climb, 60 kilometres form the finish at Orcières-Merlette, Luis Ocaña distanced Lucien Van Impe, Joaquim Agostinho and Joop Zoetemelk, while Eddy Merckx was already trailing by a couple of minutes. The Spaniard flew to victory and opened massive gaps: 5’52’’ to Van Impe, second on the day, and 8’42’’ to Merckx, who fought back to the third place. Ocaña looked set to dethrone Merckx, the winner of the 1969 and 1970 editions, but he abandoned four days later after a crash on the downhill from Col de Menté.
Most recently, Col du Noyer featured in stage 17 of the Tour 2024. Richard Carapaz distanced his rivals on the climb and soloed to victory in Superdévoluy. The Ecuadorian climber is at the front again today, after his stage win at Orcières-Merlette yesterday."],"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T14:46:56+02:00","pinned":false,"year":2026,"type":"liv","highlight":false,"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T14:46:56+02:00","stage":19,"_id":"55ec141df8589b153ebb6a5eab8c05ed788955b861c5e2edcb263a004ac7c5bb","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784897216677,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:col-du-noyer-from-ocana-to-carapaz","model":"news"},{"socialContentLangs":[{"createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","createdBy":"ganz","updatedBy":"ganz","locale":"fr","title":"Maillot blanc"},{"createdBy":"ganz","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","title":"White jersey","locale":"en","updatedBy":"ganz"},{"locale":"de","updatedBy":"ganz","title":"Weißes Trikot","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","createdBy":"ganz"}],"stage":{"timezone":"Europe/Paris","showGroups":true,"length":127.9,"podiumDisplayMode":"instantpodium","lengthDisplay":128,"stage":19,"date":"2026-07-24T00:00:00+02:00","type":"HMG","isCancelled":false},"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","highlight":false,"createdBy":"ganz","onlyDataTab":false,"onlyClubTab":false,"url":"https://www.instagram.com/p/DbK2dG-ObUh/?utm_source=ig_web_copy_link&igsh=MzRlODBiNWFlZA==","createdAt":"2026-07-24T13:41:09+02:00","id":8590,"pinned":false,"updatedBy":"ganz","embedCode":"

The riders first rolled through Gap in the early morning of 16 July 1905, on their way from Grenoble to Toulon, where Hippolyte Aucouturier took the fourth of his five Tour stage wins.
The first time the race stopped in Gap was in 1931. Jeff Demuysere won stage 15, which started in Nice. Then Charles Pélissier conquered Grenoble, after 102 kilometres of racing from Gap.Today marks the 26th stage start in Gap, the first since 2024, when Victor Campenaerts escaped to victory in Barcelonnette. The town often gave way to stages in the Alps, including three Gap - Alpe d’Huez stages in 1991, 2006, and 2013."],"createdBy":"bvittek","title":"Gap, a historic venue of the Tour","updatedBy":"bvittek","_id":"5c29dd16f28123adcc6e606aff3d870b2e141327dd2fd4dfe4722d846431ee86","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784893047421,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:gap-a-historic-venue-of-the-tour","model":"news"},{"type":"liv","highlight":false,"year":2026,"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T14:42:01+02:00","stage":19,"createdAt":"2026-07-24T14:42:01+02:00","stageType":"HMG","picto":"liv_group","locale":"en","slug":"the-leaders","title":"The 42 leaders","createdBy":"bvittek","updatedBy":"bvittek","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T14:45:41+02:00","text":["UAE Emirates RXG: Yates

The Pyrenean giant appeared on the Tour as early as 1910, while the Alpine myth waited until 1952 for its debut. But what a debut it was!
On 4 July 1952, the peloton set off from Lausanne and covered around 250 kilometres to reach Le Bourg d’Oisans and begin the final ascent. Jean Robic attacked, Fausto Coppi countered, and the Italian Campionissimo covered the last 6 kilometres alone to win the stage 1’20’’ ahead of his French rival. Sixth at the summit, race leader Andrea Carrea lost 3’29’’ that day and handed the Maillot Jaune to Coppi, the new leader by 5 seconds. He retained it all the way to Paris, where he ruled the overall standings for the second time.
The Tour didn’t return to Alpe d’Huez until 1976. On that occasion, Joop Zoetemelk wrote the first chapter of the strong relationship between the Netherlands and a summit also known as the “Dutch mountain”.
The summit then became a staple of the Tour: today marks the 32nd finish at Alpe d’Huez! It is, by far, the mountain top that has hosted the most stage finishes in Tour history. Puy de Dôme follows with 14 finishes, and then Mont Ventoux with 11.
Alpe d’Huez has also featured in the Critérium du Dauphiné and in the Tour de France Femmes avec Zwift. In 2024, it was the magnificent setting to the final stage: Demi Vollering took off to victory but Kasia Niewiadoma-Phinney sealed the overall victory by 4 seconds!"],"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T16:46:39+02:00","pinned":false,"title":"The most iconic mountain Will the Slovenian star claim another polka-dot jersey on the Champs-Élysées, as he did in 2020, 2021, and 2025?
Valentin Paret-Peintre (Soudal Quick-Step) never gave up on the target he had set for himself before the Tour de France and, after the early ascents of Col Bayard and Col du Noyer, he is up to 84 points, while Pogacar has 70. And Richard Carapaz (EF Education-EasyPost) is a close rival: 74 points.
Since the polka-dot jersey was introduced in 1975, the winner of the KOM standings took it in the last three days of racing on six occasions. Giancarlo Bellini was the first to do so, in 1976, edging Lucien Van Impe on day 21 (penultimate stage). Then it happened five times between 2011 and 2024.
In 2020, Tadej Pogacar took the polka-dot jersey from Richard Carapaz on stage 20, while the Ecuadorian took his revenge on day 19 of the Tour 2024.
This year, everything should be decided on stage 20, with a whooping 70 KOM points on offer between Le Bourg d'Oisans and Alpe d'Huez."],"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T15:12:23+02:00","pinned":false,"id":1353471,"createdAt":"2026-07-24T15:12:23+02:00","stageType":"HMG","picto":"liv_polka_dot_jersey","locale":"en","slug":"claiming-the-polka-dot-jersey-at-the-eleventh-hour","publicationAt":"2026-07-24T15:12:23+02:00","stage":19,"type":"liv","highlight":true,"year":2026,"_id":"73d840fe3d7e20ea7e5a3a8c06d5f23f5630a0c0aeec437f3c1d94f12a13dc4c","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784898743906,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:claiming-the-polka-dot-jersey-at-the-eleventh-hour","model":"news"},{"publicationAt":"2026-07-24T15:03:47+02:00","stage":19,"type":"liv","highlight":false,"year":2026,"createdBy":"bvittek","title":"Another July: Page and Kübler","updatedAt":"2026-07-24T13:45:14+02:00","pinned":false,"text":["Born on 24 July 2001 in Chartres, Hugo Page is celebrating his 25th birthday on the route of the Tour de France 2026! This is his third consecutive appearance in the race, and he recently achieved his best result to date by finishing 12th in Voiron (stage 17). Hugo Page has one professional victory to his name, secured in a sprint finish on the Tour du Limousin-Périgord – Nouvelle-Aquitaine.
Ferdinand Kübler (1919–2016) was born on 24 July in Marthalen, Switzerland. Turning professional in 1940, he had to wait until the end of the Second World War to demonstrate the full extent of his abilities, which would lead him to become the first Swiss winner of the Tour de France in 1950. He also won eight stages and the points classification (1954), was world road champion (1951), a two-time winner of Liège–Bastogne–Liège (1951–52) and La Flèche Wallonne (also 1951–52), 'Ferdi' is one of the greatest Swiss cyclists in the 13 summit finishes on this legendary mountain that took place between 1976 and 1989, eight ended in victory for a Dutch rider: the winners were Joop Zoetemelk (2), Hennie Kuiper (2), Peter Winnen (2), Steven Rooks and Gert-Jan Theunisse. 5.5 kilometres from the summit lies the legendary “Dutch bend”; the seventh of the 21 hairpin bends the riders will tackle.
Alpe d’Huez has also seen Dutch victories by Leontien van Moorsel (Tour Cycliste Féminin 1992 and 1993) and Demi Vollering (Tour de France Femmes avec Zwift 2024).
Today, one Dutch rider made the breakaway: Thymen Arensman (Netcompany Ineos), who keeps fond memories from witnessing the Tour de France on the roadside at Alpe d’Huez when he was a kid."],"pinned":false,"updatedAt":"2026-07-24T16:51:24+02:00","updatedBy":"bvittek","title":"Why is Alpe d’Huez known as ‘the Dutch mountain’?","createdBy":"bvittek","_id":"ca7980ac0075a3fc67bc6db74012c87b168578c68b1f87f7db211e94671760fa","_bind":"publication_en-2026-19","_origin":"news-2026-19","_updatedAt":1784904684578,"_parent":"stage-2026:f645bea74ffb6582aef8d9c884488fa8ccf6fae238107138735516299a39b77d","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:why-is-alpe-dhuez-known-as-the-dutch-mountain","model":"news"},{"text":["Martinez is the quickest to react. Each day, time limits for elimination are calculated based on a coefficient reflecting the stage's difficulty and the winner's average speed. Today’s stage from Gap to L’Alpe d’Huez—deemed "highly difficult" by the organizers—carries a coefficient of 5. This results in a time limit ranging from 11% to 19% of the winner's finishing time. Given the average speed since the start, the time limit is expected to be set at 19%, or approximately 35 minutes.
